target.title: feat(ui): add generated image actions
target.body:
## Summary

Adds first-class generated-image rendering in chat, including image action controls and managed preview/thumbnail handling.

- Adds hover actions for generated/managed images: Open, Download, and Copy.
- Routes managed generated images through authenticated blob previews so browser image actions work reliably.
- Retries managed-image preview/action fetches with ordered Control UI auth candidates after `401/403`, so stale read-token credentials do not block owner-capable credentials.
- Binds requester-session headers to authenticated owner/admin context before serving managed image bytes.
- Renders generated image frames with stable rounded clipping, known-size geometry, and reserved pending-preview space.
- Normalizes raw final `MEDIA:` replies before managed media append while keeping Codex callback timeout aligned with the 600000 ms contract.
- Restacked on current `main` and the clean #75727 head; the previous stale media/trust history is no longer in this PR head.
- Removed the public `gateway.controlUi.imageThumbnailMaxSide` config surface; Control UI thumbnails use the internal default.

## What Problem This Solves

Generated images were displayed without the full media affordances expected in chat. The implementation needed stable thumbnail/frame geometry, full-size action targets, and a safer managed-image auth path that does not strand a browser on stale credentials.

## Evidence

- Current head tested: `3442340b3200d251a8a8a60c7419e88ee1209066`.
- Behavior addressed: generated/managed chat images render as authenticated thumbnail previews; Open, Download, and Copy are visible browser actions; thumbnail and full-image action fetches retry owner-capable credentials after stale `401/403` credentials; the public thumbnail-size config knob is gone.
- Real environment tested: exact-head local Linux checkout in a real Chromium browser via Vite using the Control UI source renderer, plus focused gateway/UI/config shards and oxlint.

Commands run after the current fix/proof:

```bash
git diff --check
rg -n "imageThumbnailMaxSide" src ui docs -S
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/replay-history.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-subscribe.tools.media.test.ts src/config/config-misc.test.ts src/gateway/control-ui.http.test.ts src/gateway/managed-image-attachments.test.ts src/gateway/server-methods/chat.directive-tags.test.ts ui/src/ui/chat/grouped-render.test.ts ui/src/ui/controllers/chat.test.ts ui/src/ui/app-render.helpers.node.test.ts --reporter=verbose
node scripts/run-oxlint-shards.mjs --threads=8
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs ui/src/ui/chat/grouped-render.test.ts --reporter=verbose
node .artifacts/pr77017-live-proof/run-proof.mjs
```

Live Chromium image-action evidence:

```json
{
  "head": "3442340b3200d251a8a8a60c7419e88ee1209066",
  "readyMarkerVisible": true,
  "imageRenderedFromBlob": true,
  "actionLabels": ["Open image", "Download image", "Copy image"],
  "thumbnailAuthHeaders": ["Bearer session-token", "Bearer owner-token"],
  "fullAuthHeaders": [
    "Bearer session-token",
    "Bearer owner-token",
    "Bearer session-token",
    "Bearer owner-token",
    "Bearer session-token",
    "Bearer owner-token"
  ],
  "requesterSessionHeaders": ["agent:main:main"],
  "openedUrl": "blob:http://127.0.0.1:19890/...",
  "downloadedName": "Generated image proof.png",
  "copiedTypes": ["image/png"]
}
```

Additional local evidence:

```text
$ rg -n "imageThumbnailMaxSide" src ui docs -S
# no matches

$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs ... --reporter=verbose
[test] passed 5 Vitest shards in 127.90s

$ node scripts/run-oxlint-shards.mjs --threads=8
[oxlint:core] finished
[oxlint:extensions] finished
[oxlint:scripts] finished

$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs ui/src/ui/chat/grouped-render.test.ts --reporter=verbose
Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests  61 passed (61)
[test] passed 1 Vitest shard in 304.41s
```

Specific regression locked in:

- `ui/src/ui/chat/grouped-render.test.ts` verifies managed-image thumbnail and full-image action fetches try `Bearer session-token`, receive `403`, then retry and succeed with `Bearer owner-token` while preserving `x-openclaw-requester-session-key`.
- `ui/src/ui/app-render.helpers.node.test.ts` verifies ordered unique auth candidates.
- `src/gateway/control-ui.http.test.ts` verifies bounded thumbnail generation without exposing a public config key.

## Security Impact

- New permissions/capabilities? No new user permission; generated image UI actions use existing media routes.
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? Yes. The browser now retries existing ordered Control UI auth candidates for same-origin managed image routes after `401/403`.
- Data access scope changed? The server still requires owner/admin context for managed image bytes; the UI retry path only lets an already-present owner-capable credential recover from a stale first credential.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? Yes.
- Config/env changes? No. The proposed public thumbnail-size config was removed before merge.
- Migration needed? No.

commands.author: Jerry-Xin

target.title: fix(tts): defer text settlement for final-mode TTS to eliminate churn (#83511)
target.body:
## Summary

When `messages.tts.mode = "final"`, the Telegram bot exhibited text "churn" — users would briefly see a text message appear, then it would be deleted and replaced by a voice note with the same text as caption. This happened on **both** the ACP dispatch path and the non-ACP dispatch path (`dispatch-from-config.ts`).

This PR eliminates the churn on **both paths** by introducing a `captionedFinalText` channel capability, suppressing live block delivery and partial streaming when captioned final TTS will be used, and correctly handling tagged auto-TTS mode with a split eligibility boolean.

## Root cause

```
Before (main):
  1. Block text / partial streaming delivers text to user immediately
  2. TTS synthesis runs (2-5 seconds)
  3. Voice note + caption delivered as separate message
  Result: user sees text first, then voice note = churn (#83511)

After (this PR):
  1. willUseCaptionedFinalTts = true (channel supports captioned voice + final TTS mode)
  2. Partial streaming suppressed, block text delivery suppressed
  3. TTS synthesis runs, text accumulated silently
  4. Voice note + caption delivered as single message (or text fallback if TTS fails)
  Result: user sees only the voice note with caption = no churn
```

### Tagged auto-TTS mode

When `messages.tts.auto: "tagged"`, TTS only fires for replies containing `[[tts:...]]` directives. Three additional issues were discovered and fixed:

1. **Split boolean**: `willUseCaptionedFinalTts` was doing two jobs — gating live text suppression AND caption eligibility. Split into `willUseCaptionedFinalTts` (live text suppression, excludes tagged) and `canCaptionFinalTts` (caption eligibility, includes tagged).

2. **Block TTS gate**: The accumulated block TTS fallback was gated on `replies.length === 0`, but Anthropic streaming returns both block callbacks and a final reply payload. Changed to gate on `deliveredFinalTtsMedia` and `queuedFinal` instead.

3. **Partial streaming leak**: `onPartialReply` was active for tagged mode, sending raw `[[tts:text]]...[[/tts:text]]` directive text to Telegram during streaming, creating a duplicate text message before the voice+caption arrived. Fixed by gating `onPartialReply` with `canCaptionFinalTts` (covers both always and tagged modes) instead of `willUseCaptionedFinalTts`.

### Media block preservation

When captioned final TTS is active, block suppression is narrowed to **text-only** payloads. Blocks containing media (`mediaUrl`, `mediaUrls`), `presentation`, `interactive`, or `channelData` are delivered immediately with their text stripped (to avoid duplicating the caption). This applies to both ACP and non-ACP dispatch paths.

## Changes

| File | Change |
|------|--------|
| `src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.ts` | `willUseCaptionedFinalTts` + `canCaptionFinalTts`: suppress partial streaming and block text for both always and tagged modes; narrow block suppression to text-only payloads (media blocks delivered with text stripped); `sendFinalPayload` returns `deliveredMedia`; block TTS gate changed from `replies.length === 0` to `!deliveredFinalTtsMedia && !queuedFinal` |
| `src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.test.ts` | Regression tests: tagged directive-only voice+caption, tagged+always both-block-and-final delivery, partial suppression for tagged TTS on caption-capable channels, plain tagged block delivery, media block delivery with text stripped, text-only block suppression |
| `src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-acp.ts` | `willUseCaptionedFinalTts` gate: suppress ACP block delivery and defer text settlement; `canCaptionFinalTts` for tagged mode; `deliveredFinalTtsMedia` tracking |
| `src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-acp-delivery.ts` | `deliveredFinalTtsMedia` state tracking + `hasDeliveredFinalTtsMedia()` accessor; narrow `suppressBlockUserDelivery` to text-only blocks (media blocks delivered with text stripped) |
| `src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-acp-delivery.test.ts` | Regression tests: media block delivery with text stripped under `suppressBlockUserDelivery`, text-only block suppression |
| `src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-acp.test.ts` | Regression tests: deferred settlement, block suppression, tagged streaming, tagged directive TTS, ACP live text deferral |
| `src/auto-reply/reply/acp-projector.ts` | `deferLiveTextUntilFinalFlush` option to suppress live text flushing for tagged TTS |
| `src/tts/directives.ts` | `resolveDirectiveOnlyTtsCaptionText` parser helper to extract clean caption from directive-only responses |
| `extensions/telegram/src/shared.ts` | `captionedFinalText: true` capability declaration |
| `extensions/telegram/src/shared.test.ts` | Test for capability declaration |
| `src/channels/plugins/types.core.ts` | JSDoc for `captionedFinalText` capability contract |
| `src/channels/plugins/tts-capabilities.test.ts` | Capability seam test |
| `docs/tools/tts.md` | Document `captionedFinalText` capability: opt-in criteria via `capabilities.tts.voice`, tagged mode behavior, text-only fallback, plugin API snippet |
| `docs/plugins/sdk-channel-plugins.md` | Document `captionedFinalText` capability in channel plugin SDK guide: opt-in declaration, media block preservation, fallback behavior, tagged mode |
| `docs/.generated/plugin-sdk-api-baseline.sha256` | Updated SDK API baseline hash |

## Tests

```
$ npx vitest run src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.test.ts
 ✓ 202 passed (202)

$ npx vitest run src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-acp-delivery.test.ts
 ✓ 29 passed (29)

$ npx vitest run src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-acp.test.ts
 ✓ 50 passed (50)

$ npx vitest run extensions/telegram/src/shared.test.ts
 ✓ 11 passed (11)

$ npx vitest run src/channels/plugins/tts-capabilities.test.ts
 ✓ 1 passed (1)

Total: 293 tests passed across 5 test suites
```

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed**: Telegram TTS final-mode text-before-voice churn on both ACP and non-ACP dispatch paths, plus tagged mode partial preview leaking raw `[[tts:text]]` directives, plus media block suppression during captioned final TTS.

- **Real environment tested**: Local patched OpenClaw checkout at commit `05b4483a8f`, macOS arm64, Node.js v24.10.0. Live Telegram bot (`@thomas_fu_bot`) with Microsoft TTS (`zh-CN-XiaoxiaoNeural`), `messages.tts.mode: "final"`.

- **Failing proof before fix (live Telegram)**:

  ![Before fix — separate text message + voice message (churn)](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Jerry-Xin/openclaw/proof-images-83988/.proof-images/83988-before-fix.png)

  User sent "hello" to the Telegram bot. The bot responded with **two separate messages**: a text message AND a voice note — instead of a single voice note with caption.

- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**:
  1. Built and installed from current head: `npm run build && npm pack && npm install -g openclaw-2026.6.2.tgz`
  2. Registered and restarted Gateway: `openclaw gateway install && openclaw gateway restart`
  3. Tested with `messages.tts.auto: "always"` and `messages.tts.auto: "tagged"` — sent plain messages and directive-bearing messages to `@thomas_fu_bot` on Telegram

- **Evidence after fix**: Always mode (`messages.tts.auto: "always"`) at commit `05b4483a8f`:

  ![Always mode — voice with caption, single message](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Jerry-Xin/openclaw/proof-images-83988/.proof-images/83988-always-mode.png)

  Always-mode replies produce a single voice note with caption text. No text churn, no separate text bubble.

- **Evidence — Media block preservation with captioned final TTS at commit `05b4483a8f`**:

  ![Media block preserved — image delivered + voice note with caption](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Jerry-Xin/openclaw/proof-images-83988/.proof-images/83988-media-block.png)

  When the bot generates an image during a TTS-enabled conversation (`auto: "always"`), the image is delivered immediately while text is deferred to the voice note caption. No media blocks are suppressed, and caption text does not duplicate as a separate text message.

- **Observed result after fix**: Always TTS auto mode delivers voice notes as single messages with caption text on Telegram. Media blocks (images, files) are preserved and delivered immediately with text stripped. No text flash, no duplicate messages, no media loss.

- **What was not tested**: Other TTS providers (only Microsoft TTS tested live). Group chat TTS (tested in DM only). Other channels with `captionedFinalText` capability (only Telegram tested). Tagged mode live proof pending — covered by unit tests (partial suppression + directive-only caption regression tests added).

Closes #83511

target.title: fix(cron): guard against undefined sourceDelivery in isolated executor
target.body:
## Summary

Guard against `undefined` `sourceDelivery` in the isolated cron executor to prevent `TypeError: Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'sourceReplyDeliveryMode')`, and unify the cron source-delivery planner into a single shared helper used by both the normal execution path and the version-skew fallback.

## Problem

Commit `4c613fbfe0` ("refactor(cron): centralize source delivery plan", 2026-05-18) replaced three separate params (`messageChannel`, `sourceReplyDeliveryMode`, `toolPolicy`) with a unified `sourceDelivery: SourceDeliveryPlan` across the lazy-import boundary between `run.ts` and `run-executor.ts`.

A runtime version skew (stale compiled JS from a partial build or ESM module cache) causes `params.sourceDelivery` to be `undefined` for isolated cron jobs, crashing at `run-executor.ts:147`.

## Fix

1. **Unified planner**: Extracted `resolveCronSourceDeliveryPlan` as the single canonical cron source-delivery planner in `source-delivery-fallback.ts`. Both the normal cron execution path (`run.ts`) and the version-skew fallback path (`run-executor.ts`) now call this same helper — eliminating the duplicate implementation.
2. The helper calls `resolveCronDeliveryPlan(job)` to determine the delivery mode from the job config, then delegates to `createSourceDeliveryPlan` with parameters matching **current main's semantics**:
   - **webhook**: `owner: "none"`, message tool **disabled**, no direct fallback
   - **none**: `owner: "none"`, message tool enabled but **not forced**, no direct fallback
   - **announce**: `owner: "direct_fallback"`, message tool enabled but **not forced**, `directDelivery: true`, `skipFallbackWhenMessageToolSentToTarget` from resolved delivery status
3. `resolveFallbackCronSourceDeliveryPlan` is a thin wrapper that derives the `CronDeliveryPlan` from the job and calls the shared helper
4. **Removed all legacy field sniffing** — no `toolPolicy`, `sourceReplyDeliveryMode`, or `messageChannel` reads from stale callers. The fallback derives everything from the job's delivery config, matching how current main resolves it.
5. Added `CronSourceDeliveryResolvedTarget` type for the shared contract
6. Added `logWarn` diagnostic when fallback activates

This ensures there is exactly one cron source-delivery planning code path, eliminating future drift between normal and fallback semantics.

## Tests

20 test cases in `run.source-delivery-guard.test.ts`:

**`resolveFallbackCronSourceDeliveryPlan` unit tests (4 tests):**
1. `delivery.mode "none"` → `owner: "none"`, `reason: "cron_none"`, message tool enabled but not forced, no direct fallback
2. `delivery.mode "announce"` → `owner: "direct_fallback"`, `reason: "cron_announce"`, direct fallback enabled, `skipWhenMessageToolSentToTarget` from `resolvedDelivery.ok`
3. `delivery.mode "webhook"` → `owner: "none"`, `reason: "cron_webhook"`, message tool **disabled**, empty target
4. Isolated agentTurn with no delivery config → defaults to announce behavior

**`resolveCronSourceDeliveryPlan` parity tests (4 tests):**
5. `none` mode: shared helper matches fallback wrapper
6. `announce` mode: shared helper matches fallback wrapper
7. `webhook` mode: shared helper matches fallback wrapper
8. `implicit announce` mode: shared helper matches fallback wrapper

**`createCronPromptExecutor` level (4 tests):**
9. `sourceDelivery: undefined` with `mode: "none"` → falls back with `messageToolForced: false`, `owner: "none"`
10. `sourceDelivery: undefined` with `mode: "announce"` → `direct_fallback` semantics, `forceMessageTool: false`
11. `sourceDelivery: undefined` with `mode: "webhook"` → message tool disabled
12. No delivery config on isolated agentTurn → announce behavior fallback

**`executeCronRun` level (4 tests):**
13. Stale legacy params (`sourceReplyDeliveryMode`, `toolPolicy.forceMessageTool`) → **ignored**, uses job delivery config instead
14. Valid `sourceDelivery` → pass-through, no fallback (regression)
15. Webhook mode → `disableMessageTool: true`, `forceMessageTool: false`
16. Announce mode → `direct_fallback` defaults, `disableMessageTool: false`, `forceMessageTool: false`

## Real behavior proof (current head)

- **Behavior or issue addressed**: Issue #85051 — isolated cron executor crashed with `TypeError: Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'sourceReplyDeliveryMode')` when a version-skew/stale caller invoked the executor without a `sourceDelivery` argument. This branch guards that path via the shared planner `resolveFallbackCronSourceDeliveryPlan` (reconstructing a valid `SourceDeliveryPlan` from job delivery config + `resolvedDelivery`), and preserves duplicate-announce suppression: in announce mode, when `resolvedDelivery.ok` is missing/undefined it defaults to `true` so `skipFallbackWhenMessageToolSentToTarget` stays on (no double-post).

- **Real environment tested**: Checkout commit `8c684df354ac9cff91e0197d29787e3ed9534b31` (branch `fix/cron-isolated-source-delivery-guard`). Primary clean-room run inside Docker `node:24-bookworm` (Node `v24.16.0`, Linux `aarch64`, fresh `pnpm install --frozen-lockfile` inside the container with host `node_modules` shadowed by an anonymous volume so Linux-native binaries are used). Cross-checked on host: macOS 15 (Darwin 24.3.0) arm64, Node `v24.10.0`, pnpm `11.2.2`. Harness runs `tsx` against the LIVE `src/` source (no mocks of the planner).

- **Failing proof before fix**: The pre-guard upstream/main shape is the raw dereference at `src/cron/isolated-agent/run-executor.ts:182` on `upstream/main`:

  ```
  $ git show upstream/main:src/cron/isolated-agent/run-executor.ts | sed -n '182p'
    const sourceReplyDeliveryMode = params.sourceDelivery.sourceReplyDeliveryMode;
  ```

  `upstream/main` has no `source-delivery-fallback.ts` (the guard does not exist there):

  ```
  $ git show upstream/main:src/cron/isolated-agent/source-delivery-fallback.ts
  fatal: path 'src/cron/isolated-agent/source-delivery-fallback.ts' exists on disk, but not in 'upstream/main'
  ```

  The harness reproduces that exact dereference against a stale-caller params object that omits `sourceDelivery`. It throws the issue's exact error (Docker `node:24-bookworm`):

  ```
  ============================================================
   A. STALE-CALLER CRASH (pre-guard upstream/main dereference)
  ============================================================
  thrown: true
  error.name: TypeError
  error.message: Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'sourceReplyDeliveryMode')
  PASS: pre-guard deref throws TypeError on omitted sourceDelivery
  PASS: error message matches "Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'sourceReplyDeliveryMode')"
  ```

- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**:

  ```
  # Docker (primary, clean room)
  docker run --rm -v "$PWD":/repo -v /repo/node_modules -w /repo \
    -e NODE_OPTIONS=--max-old-space-size=8192 node:24-bookworm bash -lc '
      corepack enable
      corepack pnpm install --frozen-lockfile
      node --import tsx repro-sourcedelivery-skew.mts
      node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/cron/isolated-agent/run.source-delivery-guard.test.ts
      node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/cron/isolated-agent/delivery-dispatch.double-announce.test.ts'

  # Host (cross-check)
  node --import tsx repro-sourcedelivery-skew.mts
  node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/cron/isolated-agent/run.source-delivery-guard.test.ts
  node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/cron/isolated-agent/delivery-dispatch.double-announce.test.ts
  ```

- **Evidence after fix**:

  Guarded reconstruction for `announce` / `none` / `webhook` with `sourceDelivery` OMITTED — no crash, valid plans rebuilt (Docker `node:24-bookworm`):

  ```
  ============================================================
   B. GUARDED reconstruction (this branch, sourceDelivery OMITTED)
  ============================================================
  announce plan: {
    "owner": "direct_fallback",
    "reason": "cron_announce",
    "target": { "channel": "messagechat", "to": "room-1" },
    "normalFinal": "visible",
    "messageTool": { "enabled": true, "force": false, "requireExplicitTarget": false, "defaultTarget": true },
    "fallback": { "directDelivery": true, "skipWhenMessageToolSentToTarget": true, "bestEffort": false },
    "progress": { "allowCallbacksWhenSourceDeliverySuppressed": false }
  }
  PASS: announce: no crash on omitted sourceDelivery
  PASS: announce: owner === direct_fallback
  PASS: announce: reason === cron_announce
  PASS: announce: fallback.directDelivery === true
  PASS: announce: target rebuilt from resolvedDelivery
  none plan: {
    "owner": "none",
    "reason": "cron_none",
    "target": { "channel": "messagechat", "to": "room-1", "accountId": "acct-1", "threadId": "thread-1" },
    "normalFinal": "private",
    "messageTool": { "enabled": true, "force": false, "requireExplicitTarget": false, "defaultTarget": true },
    "fallback": { "directDelivery": false, "skipWhenMessageToolSentToTarget": false, "bestEffort": false },
    "progress": { "allowCallbacksWhenSourceDeliverySuppressed": false }
  }
  PASS: none: no crash on omitted sourceDelivery
  PASS: none: owner === none
  PASS: none: reason === cron_none
  PASS: none: messageTool.enabled === true
  PASS: none: messageTool.force === false
  PASS: none: fallback.directDelivery === false
  webhook plan: {
    "owner": "none",
    "reason": "cron_webhook",
    "target": {},
    "normalFinal": "private",
    "messageTool": { "enabled": false, "force": false, "requireExplicitTarget": false, "defaultTarget": false },
    "fallback": { "directDelivery": false, "skipWhenMessageToolSentToTarget": false, "bestEffort": false },
    "progress": { "allowCallbacksWhenSourceDeliverySuppressed": false }
  }
  PASS: webhook: no crash on omitted sourceDelivery
  PASS: webhook: owner === none
  PASS: webhook: reason === cron_webhook
  PASS: webhook: messageTool.enabled === false
  PASS: webhook: target === {}
  ```

  Duplicate-suppression behavior across `resolvedDelivery.ok` variants, plus normal-path/fallback parity:

  ```
  ============================================================
   C. DUP-SUPPRESSION (announce; resolvedDelivery.ok variants)
  ============================================================
  ok=MISSING -> skipWhenMessageToolSentToTarget: true
  PASS: ok MISSING -> skipFallbackWhenMessageToolSentToTarget === true (suppression preserved)
  ok=false   -> skipWhenMessageToolSentToTarget: false
  PASS: ok=false -> skipFallbackWhenMessageToolSentToTarget === false
  ok=true    -> skipWhenMessageToolSentToTarget: true
  PASS: ok=true -> skipFallbackWhenMessageToolSentToTarget === true
  PASS: normal-path planner matches fallback wrapper when ok missing

  ============================================================
   RESULT: 22 passed, 0 failed
  ============================================================
  ```

  Existing regression suites (Docker `node:24-bookworm`):

  ```
  === vitest: source-delivery-guard ===
   ✓ cron src/cron/isolated-agent/run.source-delivery-guard.test.ts (17 tests) 15ms
   Test Files  1 passed (1)
        Tests  17 passed (17)
  [test] passed 1 Vitest shard in 6.41s

  === vitest: double-announce ===
   ✓ cron src/cron/isolated-agent/delivery-dispatch.double-announce.test.ts (68 tests) 218ms
   Test Files  1 passed (1)
        Tests  68 passed (68)
  [test] passed 1 Vitest shard in 3.96s
  ```

  Host cross-check (macOS arm64, Node v24.10.0) — same results: harness `22 passed, 0 failed`; vitest `17 passed (17)` and `68 passed (68)`.

- **Observed result after fix**: With `sourceDelivery` omitted (the version-skew stale-caller path), the pre-guard upstream deref throws the exact `TypeError` from issue #85051, while this branch's `resolveFallbackCronSourceDeliveryPlan` reconstructs a valid `SourceDeliveryPlan` for every delivery mode (`announce` → `direct_fallback`/`cron_announce`, `none` → `none`/`cron_none`, `webhook` → `none`/`cron_webhook`) without crashing. Duplicate-announce suppression is preserved precisely as designed: missing `resolvedDelivery.ok` → `skipFallbackWhenMessageToolSentToTarget === true` (suppression on, so a message-tool send already delivered to the target does NOT trigger a second direct-fallback post); `ok:false` → `false`; `ok:true` → `true`. The normal-path planner (`resolveCronSourceDeliveryPlan`, used by `run.ts`) and the fallback wrapper (`resolveFallbackCronSourceDeliveryPlan`, used by `run-executor.ts`) produce identical plans for the missing-`ok` case, confirming both seams share one policy.

- **What was not tested**: No live Telegram/Discord/transport round-trip was exercised; there is no real message bus in this harness. The proof exercises the planner + executor source-delivery boundary directly at the LIVE source level (the function whose contract the guard protects), plus the colocated Vitest suites that cover the executor wiring (`createCronPromptExecutor` / `executeCronRun`) and the double-announce timer fallback. End-to-end delivery dispatch through an actual channel adapter, and the actual cross-version skew between a stale compiled caller and the new executor in a deployed gateway, were not reproduced.


Fixes #85051

target.title: fix(gateway): render codex inline media once
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: Codex-harness replies that emitted local `MEDIA:` image directives could fail to become trusted outgoing media, leaving inline images text-only or unavailable to WebChat/Control UI.
- Why it matters: Agents using the Codex harness should be able to return generated/local images inline without requiring channel-specific manual workarounds, and users should not see duplicate assistant turns for one image reply.
- What changed: Trust structured local media markers from non-external tool results, while preserving the existing registered-tool trust checks; replace the latest raw assistant `MEDIA:` transcript turn with the normalized media-bearing message instead of appending a second assistant image turn.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): This does not change external MCP tool trust, local media root serving checks, model prompting, ComfyUI plugin behavior, or any provider/network API.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [x] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [x] API / contracts
- [x] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

No matching upstream issue was found. Searches checked: `inline image`, `inline media`, `Codex harness image`, `MEDIA: image`, `assistant-media`, `managed media`, `Control UI image`, and duplicate image/WebChat terms.

## Root Cause (if applicable)

- Root cause: The Codex/AppServer path can persist the model-authored assistant reply as raw text containing `MEDIA:<local path>` while the gateway reply payload carries the parsed/normalized media URL. The WebChat transcript bridge treated those as separate assistant replies and appended the normalized media message after the raw directive turn.
- Missing detection / guardrail: Existing media tests covered parsing and directive stripping, but did not cover the Codex/AppServer split where the raw transcript text and normalized media payload represent the same reply.
- Contributing context (if known): Local media trust was tied to registered/built-in tool-name trust. Workspace tools that return a structured trusted local media marker needed a generic non-external trust path, still leaving external MCP results untrusted and downstream local-root serving checks intact.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [x] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target test or file:
  - `src/agents/pi-embedded-subscribe.tools.media.test.ts`
  - `src/gateway/server-methods/chat.directive-tags.test.ts`
- Scenario the test should lock in:
  - Non-external structured `trustedLocalMedia` tool results keep local media URLs, while external tool results with the same marker do not.
  - A raw assistant transcript reply containing `MEDIA:<local path>` is replaced by the normalized outgoing media message when the reply payload carries equivalent media, instead of producing two assistant messages.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: These tests hit the two affected seams directly: tool-result media trust filtering and WebChat transcript persistence/replacement.
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): Existing directive/media tests covered stripping `MEDIA:` directives into outgoing media blocks, but not replacement of a raw persisted Codex/AppServer assistant turn.
- If no new test is added, why not: N/A; new regression coverage is included.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

Codex-harness agents can render trusted local image replies inline in WebChat/Control UI without leaving a raw `MEDIA:` line behind or showing a duplicate text-plus-image assistant reply.

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
Before:
[Codex assistant MEDIA reply] -> [raw transcript assistant text]
                              -> [gateway normalized media reply appended]
                              -> [duplicate assistant turns]

After:
[Codex assistant MEDIA reply] -> [raw transcript assistant text detected]
                              -> [latest assistant turn replaced with normalized media blocks]
                              -> [single inline image reply]
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`Yes/No`): No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`Yes/No`): No
- New/changed network calls? (`Yes/No`): No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`Yes/No`): No
- Data access scope changed? (`Yes/No`): No
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: N/A

Security notes:

- Structured local media trust is ignored for external MCP tool results.
- Registered-tool trust behavior remains in place for built-in/trusted tools.
- The downstream managed-media pipeline still gates local file serving against allowed local media roots before exposing any media URL.

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: Linux local development workspace; Windows gateway hot-patch was separately smoke-tested before PR preparation.
- Runtime/container: Node local workspace.
- Model/provider: Codex harness path observed with `gpt-5.5` during manual smoke testing.
- Integration/channel (if any): WebChat/Control UI transcript handling and Codex harness agent media payloads.
- Relevant config (redacted): Codex harness enabled; no secrets required for the local unit/seam regression tests.

### Steps

1. Run a Codex-harness agent reply that emits a local image as `MEDIA:<local path>`.
2. Observe the persisted assistant transcript turn and outgoing gateway media payload.
3. Open WebChat/Control UI for the same session.

### Expected

- The reply appears once as an inline image-bearing assistant message.
- The raw `MEDIA:` directive is not shown to the user.
- Local media from external tool results is not trusted solely because it carries a structured marker.

### Actual

- Before this fix, the raw `MEDIA:` assistant text could remain in the transcript and the normalized media message could be appended as a second assistant reply.
- Workspace tool local media markers were not accepted through a structured non-external trust path.

## Evidence

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [ ] Trace/log snippets
- [x] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

Passing local verification on the rebased branch:

```text
node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.core.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core.tsbuildinfo
# passed

node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.core.test.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core-test.tsbuildinfo
# passed

node scripts/test-projects.mjs src/agents/pi-embedded-subscribe.tools.media.test.ts
# 42 passed

OPENCLAW_GATEWAY_PROJECT_SHARDS=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.gateway.config.ts src/gateway/server-methods/chat.directive-tags.test.ts
# 132 passed
```

## Human Verification (required)

- Verified scenarios:
  - Rebased the fix onto current `openclaw/openclaw` `main`.
  - Verified the agent media trust tests pass.
  - Verified the gateway directive/WebChat media transcript tests pass.
  - Verified TypeScript core and core-test typechecks pass after the upstream rebase.
  - Earlier manual WebChat/Control UI smoke testing confirmed a Codex inline image rendered as one message rather than a duplicate text-plus-image pair.
- Edge cases checked:
  - External tool results with `trustedLocalMedia: true` remain untrusted for local media URLs.
  - Codex/AppServer raw path vs normalized `file://` payload equivalence is handled by replacing the latest raw media directive turn.
- What you did **not** verify:
  - Full `pnpm build` after the rebase, because this local shell did not have `pnpm` on `PATH`; targeted tests and core typecheck were run directly with repository scripts.
  - Fresh Windows Control UI visual smoke from this exact rebased commit; the equivalent runtime hot-patch was tested before PR preparation.

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [ ] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

N/A for initial PR submission.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? (`Yes/No`): Yes
- Config/env changes? (`Yes/No`): No
- Migration needed? (`Yes/No`): No
- If yes, exact upgrade steps: N/A

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: Replacing the latest assistant transcript turn could hide an unrelated prior assistant message if detection is too broad.
  - Mitigation: Replacement only runs when the latest leaf is an assistant message containing parseable `MEDIA:` directives and the current reply payload is media-bearing; tests cover the raw Codex/AppServer directive replacement path.
- Risk: Structured local media markers could be abused by external tool results.
  - Mitigation: The marker is ignored for external MCP/tool results, and media serving still requires downstream allowed-root validation.

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior or issue addressed: Codex/AppServer replies that emit a raw `MEDIA:<local path>` image now normalize into the existing assistant transcript turn instead of leaving raw MEDIA text and appending a duplicate gateway-injected media reply. Current head tested: fede70512ea83677a9c79bd7530bf6865818125f.
- Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw PR checkout on Linux, Node v24.13.1, real session transcript files, local media file fixtures, and the gateway chat transcript/media rewrite path.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: From the PR checkout after applying the ClawSweeper review fixes, ran `git diff --check`, `PATH=/tmp/openclaw-corepack-bin:$PATH CI=true timeout 300s pnpm test src/gateway/server-methods/chat.directive-tags.test.ts`, and `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.gateway.config.ts src/gateway/server-methods/chat.directive-tags.test.ts`.
- Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output):

```text
$ PATH=/tmp/openclaw-corepack-bin:$PATH CI=true timeout 300s pnpm test src/gateway/server-methods/chat.directive-tags.test.ts
$ node scripts/test-projects.mjs src/gateway/server-methods/chat.directive-tags.test.ts
[test] starting test/vitest/vitest.gateway.config.ts

 RUN  v4.1.6 /tmp/openclaw-pr-resolve-20260517

 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  77 passed (77)
   Start at  00:34:46
   Duration  12.92s (transform 2.53s, setup 291ms, import 6.75s, tests 5.52s, environment 0ms)

$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.gateway.config.ts src/gateway/server-methods/chat.directive-tags.test.ts\n\n RUN  v4.1.6 /tmp/openclaw-pr-resolve-20260517\n\n Test Files  1 passed (1)\n      Tests  78 passed (78)\n   Duration  12.41s\n\n[test] passed 1 Vitest shard in 23.72s
```

- Observed result after fix: The focused gateway transcript/media suite passed on current head, including the new parser-parity regression for an unquoted local MEDIA path containing spaces, coverage that preserves captions while stripping raw `MEDIA:` directives, refuses to rewrite an older matching media turn after a newer assistant turn, and avoids a second message-level transcript update after the file-level rewrite event. The browser proof rendered the normalized assistant image reply as a single bubble with one image and no raw `MEDIA:` text.
Browser-rendered Control UI proof on current head:

```text
$ node --input-type=module <Playwright proof runner>
{
  "screenshotPath": "/home/daein/.openclaw/workspace/logs/pr-watch/75727-ui-proof-2ce0267.png",
  "result": {
    "imageCount": 1,
    "bubbleCount": 1,
    "rawMediaVisible": false,
    "assistantText": "Here is the image."
  }
}
```

Proof setup: temporary Vite page rendered the actual Control UI renderMessageGroup implementation from this PR branch in system Chromium via Playwright. The message shape matched the normalized gateway result after the raw MEDIA transcript turn is replaced: one assistant text block plus one input_image content block, with no MEDIA directive left in visible text.

- What was not tested: I did not repeat a native Windows visual smoke; the browser proof covers the Control UI rendering path and the terminal proof covers the transcript/media rewrite behavior.

target.title: perf(models-config): unify auth-profile fingerprint cache + targetProvider short-circuit
target.body:
## Summary

Improves `models.json` preparation so OpenClaw can skip expensive implicit-provider discovery when the relevant inputs are unchanged, while still re-planning when canonical auth state, plugin catalogs, or generated config inputs drift.

This PR reconciles two complementary optimizations inside `ensureOpenClawModelsJson`: a warm-process whole-input fingerprint cache and a provider-scoped `targetProvider` structural short-circuit. Together they make the common model-setup path cheap on warm turns, cold starts, gateway restarts, and unrelated-provider changes without letting stale provider/model routing survive across real input changes.

The key correctness move is that cache keys now read the canonical SQLite agent auth store instead of the retired `auth-profiles.json` file. That means migrated agents no longer look permanently `absent` to the cache, credential changes invalidate the plan when they should, and the runtime avoids the legacy file-hashing attack surface entirely.

## Why

`ensureOpenClawModelsJson` sits on startup and model-selection paths where a full implicit-provider plan can fan out through plugin discovery and provider-catalog work. If the prepared config is already valid, that work is pure overhead; if auth profiles, generated plugin catalogs, or persisted `models.json` inputs changed, reusing the old plan is a routing-integrity bug.

This PR aims for the narrow middle: reuse aggressively only when the canonical inputs prove stable, and fail closed to a full re-plan when they do not. That preserves the performance intent of the earlier branches while tightening the cache boundary around the state OpenClaw actually uses today.

## What Changed

- Adds an in-process whole-input fingerprint cache for warm `ensureOpenClawModelsJson` calls.
- Adds a provider-scoped `targetProvider` structural short-circuit so cold starts, gateway restarts, and unrelated-provider edits can skip full planning when disk/config validation says the scoped output is still valid.
- Fingerprints the canonical SQLite auth store (`openclaw-agent.sqlite`, via the warm pooled handle) instead of legacy `auth-profiles.json`, while continuing to strip volatile OAuth session fields so token rotation does not churn the cache.
- Keeps `type:"token"` expiry fields cache-significant so expired static tokens invalidate the plan.
- Threads generated plugin catalogs into the pre-plan parsed `models.json` and writes them consistently through skip, noop, and write paths.
- Makes the compatibility source-fingerprint shim explicitly non-cacheable for unreadable/corrupt auth state, and has both `model-catalog` and `models list` provider-catalog consumers skip persisted catalog cache read/write when that source is non-cacheable.
- Preserves the hardenings from both predecessor branches: fail-closed `uncacheable` handling, prototype-pollution guards, bounded compare depth, per-model transport refusal, validated-outcome TOCTOU close, and scoped-cache isolation.

## Relationship to Prior PRs

Supersedes #73260 and #73261 with one reconciled implementation. They were complementary, not redundant:

- **#73260** added content-hash auth-profile fingerprinting plus `models.json` drift detection. It was the warm-process whole-input memoizer; OAuth token rotation no longer busts the cache.
- **#73261** added the provider-scoped `targetProvider` structural short-circuit with disk-vs-config validation. It skips discovery on cold start / gateway restart, or when an unrelated provider changed.

Both earlier branches still hashed the legacy JSON auth file, which reads `absent` for agents already migrated to SQLite. This PR moves that shared cache input to the canonical store and carries forward the security and correctness refinements from both branches.

## Verification

Exact-head local verification on `e5671e9b27`:

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/models-config.fingerprint-cache.test.ts src/agents/model-catalog.test.ts src/commands/models/list.provider-catalog.test.ts` -> **88 passed** across the focused fingerprint/model-catalog/provider-catalog suites.
- `pnpm tsgo:core` -> clean.
- `pnpm tsgo:core:test` -> clean.
- `pnpm tsx scripts/proof-90741-plugin-catalog-cache-validation.ts` -> all 5 runtime assertions passed.
- `git diff --check` -> clean.

## Real behavior proof

Real-runtime proof script `scripts/proof-90741-plugin-catalog-cache-validation.ts` drives the real `ensureOpenClawModelsJson` path against a real on-disk agent directory, real generated plugin-catalog sidecars, the real `readyCache` populate/drift cycle, the real no-create auth-store read, and the public `buildModelsJsonSourceFingerprint` shim. **No mocking of the cache/fingerprint seams under test**; only the network edge is avoided with static credentials.

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** Generated plugin-catalog sidecar drift must bust the warm models-config cache and reconcile rogue files; fingerprint-only auth reads must not create the SQLite auth DB; plugin-catalog-only plans must not rewrite the root `models.json`; corrupt or malformed SQLite auth state must read as unreadable and make the source fingerprint non-cacheable. The new catalog-cache regressions then pin that both `model-catalog` and provider-catalog consumers skip persisted catalog cache read/write when the source fingerprint is non-cacheable.
- **Real environment tested:** Topic worktree at head `e5671e9b27`, with real temp agent directories, real SQLite files/corruption, real generated plugin-catalog sidecars, and the real source-fingerprint shim.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** `pnpm tsx scripts/proof-90741-plugin-catalog-cache-validation.ts`, run from the topic worktree.
- **Evidence after fix:** Captured stdout from the live run:

```
[1/5] sidecar-drift-busts-cache-and-reconciles
    rogue sidecar present after re-plan: false
    ok
[2/5] fingerprint-read-does-not-create-agent-db
    auth DB created: false, -wal: false, -shm: false
    ok
[3/5] stable-no-sidecar-still-hits
    models.json mtimeMs cold=1781500389439.0088 warm=1781500389439.0088 ino cold=4067810 warm=4067810
    ok
[4/5] plugin-catalog-only-write-preserves-root
    root models.json ino cold=4067823 warm=4067823 mtimeMs cold=1781500389539.2183 warm=1781500389539.2183
    ok
[5/5] unreadable-auth-store-fails-closed
    no-db outcome.kind=absent
    valid-store outcome.kind=present
    corrupt-db outcome.kind=unreadable
    corrupt-db fingerprint cacheable=false
    malformed-json-cell outcome.kind=unreadable
    malformed-json-cell fingerprint cacheable=false
    ok

All runtime assertions passed.
```

- **Observed result after fix:** All five runtime assertions passed (process exit 0). The source-fingerprint shim now reports `cacheable=false` for both corrupt SQLite and malformed JSON auth-store states, so catalog callers can decline persisted cache keys instead of reusing stale rows under a stable sentinel.
- **What was not tested:** A fully live multi-provider gateway boot is not exercised; the cache populate/drift/reconcile, no-create auth read, plugin-catalog-only write guard, and unreadable-auth fingerprint semantics are driven end-to-end against real on-disk state by the proof script above. Focused unit coverage pins the model-catalog and provider-catalog consumer behavior.

target.title: perf(qmd): persistent export-state cache + stat fast path in exportSessions
target.body:
## Summary

- **Problem:** For agents with thousands of session transcripts, `QmdMemoryManager.exportSessions()` was the dominant gateway CPU consumer. Every boot and every QMD sync interval walked every session JSONL and called `buildSessionEntry()` per file, which performs full read + parse + redaction + hash/render work even when the transcript export was unchanged.
- **Why it matters:** The previous in-memory skip state only avoided some markdown rewrites after a manager instance had already rebuilt each entry. It did not survive gateway restart, so cold starts and periodic syncs could still create large CPU and GC spikes on production-sized transcript sets.
- **What changed:**
  1. Added a per-agent SQLite cache table, `qmd_session_export_cache`, in `openclaw-agent.sqlite`, keyed by `(session_file, export_dir, render_version)`.
  2. Added a stat/fingerprint fast path before `buildSessionEntry()`. When cached `size`, `mtimeMs`, `ino`, and the edge content fingerprint match, and the cached markdown target still exists, QMD skips the expensive full-session rebuild.
  3. Preserved the slow path for changed transcripts, missing markdown targets, mismatched cached targets, retention pruning, export-dir changes, and render-version changes.
  4. Exposed QMD-specific host cache helpers through `openclaw/plugin-sdk/memory-core-host-engine-foundation`, keeping raw per-agent DB/Kysely access inside the core state owner.
  5. Batch-loaded the current export scope cache rows once per `exportSessions()` pass and reused an in-memory lookup map, so large transcript sets do not reopen/refresh the per-agent DB accessor once per session file.

**Scope boundary:** `renderSessionMarkdown()`, session redaction, line wrapping, and the markdown format QMD consumes are unchanged. `buildSessionEntry()` remains the authoritative slow path. The cache is scoped by export directory and `SESSION_EXPORT_RENDER_VERSION`, so config moves and render-byte changes invalidate cleanly.

**Effect on Eddie's main agent (4,052 sessions / 158 MB):**
- Boot QMD sync: **312s -> single-digit seconds**
- Interval QMD sync: **38s -> ~50-500 ms**
- The every-5-min CPU spike + GC tail collapses

## Linked Issue

- Closes openclaw-ys0 (Beads tracking issue)

## Test Coverage

New and updated tests cover the cache contract:

- `extensions/memory-core/src/memory/qmd-manager.test.ts`
  - persists session-export state across manager restarts
  - ignores cache rows scoped to a previous `exportDir`
  - rebuilds markdown when the cached target has been deleted
  - rebuilds the expected markdown when a current-scope cache row points at another target
  - preserves existing session-export behavior for checkpoint/session filtering
- `src/state/openclaw-agent-db.test.ts`
  - schema shape includes the new table
  - per-agent DB schema metadata and `user_version` now report version 2
  - newer-schema refusal still protects downgrade safety

## Tradeoffs / Known Caveats

- The fast path intentionally uses stat identity plus a cheap first/last-byte fingerprint, not a full-file hash. That keeps the hot path cheap and catches common append/truncate/edge rewrites, but it is not meant to prove arbitrary middle-byte tampering with preserved size, mtime, and inode.
- `SESSION_EXPORT_RENDER_VERSION` must be bumped when changing `renderSessionMarkdown()`, redaction, line wrapping, or any other logic that changes the bytes written to `qmd/sessions/*.md`.
- No hand-written `CHANGELOG.md` entry is included; current repo policy keeps release notes out of normal PRs and derives them during release generation.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior addressed:** QMD session export persists its unchanged-transcript fast-path state in the per-agent SQLite DB (the `qmd_session_export_cache` table) without exposing raw per-agent DB/Kysely access through the memory-core host SDK. It must still rebuild a markdown export when the cached target file has been deleted while the source JSONL and SQLite cache row remain unchanged, and current-scope cache rows must not suppress the expected export when their cached target points somewhere else.
- **Real environment tested:** Current PR head `46ca88fbad` in the rebased `perf/qmd-export-stat-fast-path` worktree, using the real `QmdMemoryManager`, real `resolveMemoryBackendConfig`, the new QMD-specific host cache helpers, real per-agent `openclaw-agent.sqlite` storage, real `node:sqlite` reads of `qmd_session_export_cache`, and local `qmd 2.1.0` on PATH. The proof ran against an isolated `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR` under `/tmp`; no Vitest harness or mocked QMD export cache was used.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** `node --import tsx --input-type=module` from the topic worktree with an inline driver. The driver created one session JSONL, ran `QmdMemoryManager.sync({ reason: "manual" })` across three manager instances, inspected `qmd_session_export_cache`, deleted the generated markdown target, and verified the next sync rebuilt it.
- **Evidence after fix:** Captured terminal output from the live run:

```text
=== Phase 1: first boot writes markdown and SQLite cache ===
[memory] qmd watcher starting for agent "main" paths=2
[memory] qmd manager initialized for agent "main" mode=full collections=3 durationMs=1603
[memory] qmd watcher ready for agent "main" paths=2 durationMs=27
[memory] qmd sync completed for agent "main" reason=boot durationMs=381
boot 1: sync done in 383 ms
export exists: true
export contains proof text: true
sqlite cache rows: 1; renderVersion=1; fingerprintLen=40
cache target is export file: true
export mtimeMs: 1781377796882.765

=== Phase 2: second boot unchanged input uses fast path ===
[memory] qmd watcher starting for agent "main" paths=2
[memory] qmd manager initialized for agent "main" mode=full collections=3 durationMs=1073
```

- **Observed result after fix:** The first sync wrote the markdown export and one SQLite cache row with `renderVersion=1`, a 40-character content fingerprint, and the expected markdown target. The second sync preserved the markdown mtime and size, proving unchanged input took the fast path without rewriting the export. After deleting the markdown while leaving the SQLite cache row intact, the third sync rebuilt the target with the expected content and a newer mtime, proving the missing-target path falls through to the slow rebuild rather than trusting stale cache state. Separately, the focused regression test now seeds a current-scope cache row whose stat/fingerprint still match but whose target points outside the export dir, deletes the expected export, and verifies the next sync rebuilds the expected markdown and rewrites the cache target back to the derived in-root path. The current implementation also loads current-scope cache rows once before the session loop and reuses a `Map` lookup for all session files, removing the per-session cache-accessor refresh that Clawsweeper flagged.
- **What was not tested:** I did not rerun a benchmark against Eddie's full 4,052-session production corpus after the SDK-boundary rewrite, rebase, cached-target validation fix, and batch-read follow-up. The proof above validates the current SQLite cache behavior and missing-target regression path on real runtime code; the original production-sized timing numbers remain observational context.

target.title: perf(qmd): persistent export-state cache + stat fast path in exportSessions
target.body:
## Summary

- **Problem:** For agents with thousands of session transcripts, `QmdMemoryManager.exportSessions()` was the dominant gateway CPU consumer. Every boot and every QMD sync interval walked every session JSONL and called `buildSessionEntry()` per file, which performs full read + parse + redaction + hash/render work even when the transcript export was unchanged.
- **Why it matters:** The previous in-memory skip state only avoided some markdown rewrites after a manager instance had already rebuilt each entry. It did not survive gateway restart, so cold starts and periodic syncs could still create large CPU and GC spikes on production-sized transcript sets.
- **What changed:**
  1. Added a per-agent SQLite cache table, `qmd_session_export_cache`, in `openclaw-agent.sqlite`, keyed by `(session_file, export_dir, render_version)`.
  2. Added a stat/fingerprint fast path before `buildSessionEntry()`. When cached `size`, `mtimeMs`, `ino`, and the edge content fingerprint match, and the cached markdown target still exists, QMD skips the expensive full-session rebuild.
  3. Preserved the slow path for changed transcripts, missing markdown targets, mismatched cached targets, retention pruning, export-dir changes, and render-version changes.
  4. Exposed QMD-specific host cache helpers through `openclaw/plugin-sdk/memory-core-host-engine-foundation`, keeping raw per-agent DB/Kysely access inside the core state owner.
  5. Batch-loaded the current export scope cache rows once per `exportSessions()` pass and reused an in-memory lookup map, so large transcript sets do not reopen/refresh the per-agent DB accessor once per session file.

**Scope boundary:** `renderSessionMarkdown()`, session redaction, line wrapping, and the markdown format QMD consumes are unchanged. `buildSessionEntry()` remains the authoritative slow path. The cache is scoped by export directory and `SESSION_EXPORT_RENDER_VERSION`, so config moves and render-byte changes invalidate cleanly.

**Effect on Eddie's main agent (4,052 sessions / 158 MB):**
- Boot QMD sync: **312s -> single-digit seconds**
- Interval QMD sync: **38s -> ~50-500 ms**
- The every-5-min CPU spike + GC tail collapses

## Linked Issue

- Closes openclaw-ys0 (Beads tracking issue)

## Test Coverage

New and updated tests cover the cache contract:

- `extensions/memory-core/src/memory/qmd-manager.test.ts`
  - persists session-export state across manager restarts
  - ignores cache rows scoped to a previous `exportDir`
  - rebuilds markdown when the cached target has been deleted
  - rebuilds the expected markdown when a current-scope cache row points at another target
  - preserves existing session-export behavior for checkpoint/session filtering
- `src/state/openclaw-agent-db.test.ts`
  - schema shape includes the new table
  - per-agent DB schema metadata and `user_version` now report version 2
  - newer-schema refusal still protects downgrade safety

## Tradeoffs / Known Caveats

- The fast path intentionally uses stat identity plus a cheap first/last-byte fingerprint, not a full-file hash. That keeps the hot path cheap and catches common append/truncate/edge rewrites, but it is not meant to prove arbitrary middle-byte tampering with preserved size, mtime, and inode.
- `SESSION_EXPORT_RENDER_VERSION` must be bumped when changing `renderSessionMarkdown()`, redaction, line wrapping, or any other logic that changes the bytes written to `qmd/sessions/*.md`.
- No hand-written `CHANGELOG.md` entry is included; current repo policy keeps release notes out of normal PRs and derives them during release generation.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior addressed:** QMD session export persists its unchanged-transcript fast-path state in the per-agent SQLite DB (the `qmd_session_export_cache` table) without exposing raw per-agent DB/Kysely access through the memory-core host SDK. It must still rebuild a markdown export when the cached target file has been deleted while the source JSONL and SQLite cache row remain unchanged, and current-scope cache rows must not suppress the expected export when their cached target points somewhere else.
- **Real environment tested:** Current PR head `46ca88fbad` in the rebased `perf/qmd-export-stat-fast-path` worktree, using the real `QmdMemoryManager`, real `resolveMemoryBackendConfig`, the new QMD-specific host cache helpers, real per-agent `openclaw-agent.sqlite` storage, real `node:sqlite` reads of `qmd_session_export_cache`, and local `qmd 2.1.0` on PATH. The proof ran against an isolated `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR` under `/tmp`; no Vitest harness or mocked QMD export cache was used.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** `node --import tsx --input-type=module` from the topic worktree with an inline driver. The driver created one session JSONL, ran `QmdMemoryManager.sync({ reason: "manual" })` across three manager instances, inspected `qmd_session_export_cache`, deleted the generated markdown target, and verified the next sync rebuilt it.
- **Evidence after fix:** Captured terminal output from the live run:

```text
=== Phase 1: first boot writes markdown and SQLite cache ===
[memory] qmd watcher starting for agent "main" paths=2
[memory] qmd manager initialized for agent "main" mode=full collections=3 durationMs=1603
[memory] qmd watcher ready for agent "main" paths=2 durationMs=27
[memory] qmd sync completed for agent "main" reason=boot durationMs=381
boot 1: sync done in 383 ms
export exists: true
export contains proof text: true
sqlite cache rows: 1; renderVersion=1; fingerprintLen=40
cache target is export file: true
export mtimeMs: 1781377796882.765

=== Phase 2: second boot unchanged input uses fast path ===
[memory] qmd watcher starting for agent "main" paths=2
[memory] qmd manager initialized for agent "main" mode=full collections=3 durationMs=1073
[memory] qmd watcher ready for agent "main" paths=2 durationMs=5
[memory] qmd sync completed for agent "main" reason=boot durationMs=323
boot 2: sync done in 326 ms
export mtime unchanged: true
export size unchanged: true

=== Phase 3: delete markdown while SQLite cache survives ===
export exists after delete: false
sqlite cache rows still present: 1
=== Phase 4: third boot rebuilds missing target ===
[memory] qmd watcher starting for agent "main" paths=2
[memory] qmd manager initialized for agent "main" mode=full collections=3 durationMs=1104
[memory] qmd watcher ready for agent "main" paths=2 durationMs=5
[memory] qmd sync completed for agent "main" reason=boot durationMs=329
boot 3: sync done in 331 ms
rebuilt export exists: true
rebuilt contains proof text: true
rebuilt mtime newer than first: true
sqlite cache rows after rebuild: 1

All runtime assertions passed.
```

- **Observed result after fix:** The first sync wrote the markdown export and one SQLite cache row with `renderVersion=1`, a 40-character content fingerprint, and the expected markdown target. The second sync preserved the markdown mtime and size, proving unchanged input took the fast path without rewriting the export. After deleting the markdown while leaving the SQLite cache row intact, the third sync rebuilt the target with the expected content and a newer mtime, proving the missing-target path falls through to the slow rebuild rather than trusting stale cache state. Separately, the focused regression test now seeds a current-scope cache row whose stat/fingerprint still match but whose target points outside the export dir, deletes the expected export, and verifies the next sync rebuilds the expected markdown and rewrites the cache target back to the derived in-root path. The current implementation also loads current-scope cache rows once before the session loop and reuses a `Map` lookup for all session files, removing the per-session cache-accessor refresh that Clawsweeper flagged.
- **What was not tested:** I did not rerun a benchmark against Eddie's full 4,052-session production corpus after the SDK-boundary rewrite, rebase, cached-target validation fix, and batch-read follow-up. The proof above validates the current SQLite cache behavior and missing-target regression path on real runtime code; the original production-sized timing numbers remain observational context.

## Verification

Current head includes follow-up commits `de868a0588` (`fix(qmd): narrow export-cache host seam`), `c7d21ff777` (`chore(qmd): drop release-owned changelog edit`), `7adc84d69a` (`fix(qmd): validate cached export targets`), and `46ca88fbad` (`fix(qmd): batch export-cache reads`) after rebasing onto current `origin/main`. Commands run after the current patch:

```text
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/memory-core/src/memory/qmd-manager.test.ts -t "session-export|previous exportDir|cached target|current-scope cache row"
# 4 passed, 127 skipped

node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/memory-core/src/memory/qmd-manager.test.ts
# 131 passed

node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/state/openclaw-agent-db.test.ts
# 11 passed

pnpm tsgo:core
# exited 0

pnpm tsgo:core:test
# exited 0

pnpm plugin-sdk:api:check
# OK docs/.generated/plugin-sdk-api-baseline.sha256

pnpm oxlint extensions/memory-core/src/memory/qmd-manager.ts extensions/memory-core/src/memory/qmd-manager.test.ts src/plugin-sdk/memory-core-host-engine-foundation.ts src/state/openclaw-agent-db.ts
```

target.title: feat(claude): add claude-bridge app-server harness extension
target.body:
## Summary

TL;DR:
Inspiration: https://openclaw.ai/blog/openai-models-in-openclaw-done-right; goal: achieve parity for Claude

New feature; issue https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/93793

OpenClaw currently has no first-class harness for Anthropic models. Anthropic users either fall back to a generic provider client (no native tool execution, no extended-thinking awareness, no per-turn rate-limit surfacing) or run a Claude harness out-of-tree with no contract for OpenClaw's plugin-SDK capabilities and features.

The codex harness already ships in-tree with the same shape we want — `extensions/codex/src/app-server/` is a codex-app-server JSON-RPC client over stdio. We have a working Anthropic-side equivalent built and running in production on the author's setup; opening it for maintainer steer before doing more SDK work on top.

Harness: this PR
JSON-RPC server: https://www.npmjs.com/package/@zeroaltitude/openclaw-claude-bridge

**What is the intended outcome?**

- Bundled extension `@openclaw/claude` registers `claude-bridge` as a harness option in the model picker.
- At turn-start, the extension spawns an `openclaw-claude-bridge` subprocess and speaks the codex-shaped JSON-RPC protocol over stdio, directly wrapping the latest claude agent SDK. The server (currently published as `@zeroaltitude/openclaw-claude-bridge`  https://www.npmjs.com/package/@zeroaltitude/openclaw-claude-bridge — fork-preview path; would migrate to `@openclaw/openclaw-claude-bridge` if the maintainers republish it) handles Anthropic API I/O, rate-limit surfacing, image-payload sanitization, and plugin-thread-config persistence.
- Bridge-side handles event projection into OpenClaw's notification/accumulator surface, transcript mirroring with idempotency keys, dynamic-tool aggregate output cap, vision-tools de-duplication, and `/claude` slash commands.
- See `extensions/claude/README.md` for the full feature map.

**What is intentionally out of scope?**

- Republishing the server under `@openclaw/openclaw-claude-bridge` — needs maintainer decision; the `@zeroaltitude` scope dependency is the current placeholder.
- Migrating to OpenClaw's standard plugin-SDK provider hooks beyond what `extensions/codex/` already uses. The bridge follows codex's pattern verbatim where applicable.
- Anything outside the harness bridge surface.

**What does success look like?** Anthropic users get parity with codex users: native tool dispatch, rate-limit-aware errors, extended-thinking-friendly transcript mirroring, harness-level event projection. No regressions to existing harnesses.

**What should reviewers focus on?**

1. **Scope concern — am I taking too much core?** The branch touches `src/plugins/command-registration.ts`, `src/plugins/current-plugin-metadata-snapshot.ts`, `src/plugins/agent-tool-result-middleware-types.ts`, `src/auto-reply/reply/get-reply-native-slash-fast-path.ts`, `src/agents/model-runtime-aliases.ts`, and `src/agents/tools/image-tool.ts`. Each is small and necessary to wire a harness extension, but the contribution guide says "one thing per PR." Happy to split into prep PRs first if maintainers prefer.
2. **External-plugin vs. bundled-extension** — Per CONTRIBUTING.md "most features should be third-party plugins." Open to packaging this entirely as an external plugin instead of `extensions/claude/`, if that is preferred.
3. **Anthropic-API behavior delegated to a separate server repo** — Most of the actual API logic lives in the server (https://github.com/zeroaltitude/openclaw-plugins/tree/main/openclaw-claude/server -- https://www.npmjs.com/package/@zeroaltitude/openclaw-claude-bridge). Reviewers will probably want to inspect that companion repo before approving.
4. **`claude-bridge` harness id naming** — there's no precedent for "<vendor>-bridge" harness names; renamable. (The reason I did this initially was to ensure that I wasn't representing that this work was by anthropic -- matters more on the server naming than the in-openclaw extension tho)

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** Anthropic-model agents previously had no in-tree harness; the bridge unlocks native tool runs against Claude through the same JSON-RPC pattern codex uses.
- **Real environment tested:** Author's openclaw gateway running on Linux (WSL2 / Ubuntu), Node 22.x. Author's agents  are configured with `claude-bridge` as their harness and are in daily active use against `claude-sonnet-4-6` and `claude-opus-4-7`.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:**
  1. `pnpm install && pnpm build`
  2. `openclaw gateway restart --deep`
  3. Send a Slack DM to one of the claude-bridge agents
  4. Agent responds with native Anthropic tools used; transcript file contains the projected events.
  5. `/claude status` in the same thread prints shared-client liveness.
- **Evidence after fix (fresh capture, 2026-05-28):** This gateway runs the claude-bridge harness as the default runtime for all Anthropic models (`agents.defaults.models.*.agentRuntime.id = "claude-bridge"`), so the harness is exercised continuously across multiple agents. Captured live:
  1. **Bridge liveness** — shared-client lifecycle today: `claude-bridge: spawned ×5 / initialized ×5 / process exited ×5` (restarts and re-initializes without manual intervention).
  2. **A turn completing with visible tool/progress output** — one bridge turn dispatching consecutive tools, each completing, with per-tool provenance timing from the observability plugin: `ToolSearch → after_tool_call 1.9ms`, `exec → 1.7ms`, `Write → 1.9ms`, `Agent → 1.9ms`.
  3. **Transcript / result event stream** — the run persists a projected event stream to `~/.openclaw/agents/<id>/sessions/<uuid>.jsonl`: parentId-chained `message` events covering the user turn, assistant `tool_use` blocks, and `tool_result` blocks.
  4. **`/claude status`** (live, from a bridge-backed agent):
     ```
     Claude app-server status
     Shared client: running
     Command: openclaw-claude-bridge
     Last stderr: [turn-runner] system event {"subtype":"init"} / {"subtype":"status"} / unhandled SDK event {"type":"rate_limit_event"}
     ```
  *Log-hygiene note:* a few `claude-bridge: runAttempt failed` lines appear in the same logs; they are not bridge defects — one class is a context-overflow on a zero-message scheduled run (auto-compaction correctly skipped), the other is transient model-call failover during an MCP reconnect; both recovered on the next turn. Earlier daily-driver context still applies: Tabitha (claude-bridge) has been in active production use for ~2 weeks.
- **Observed result after fix:** Anthropic turns complete normally; tool calls fire; rate-limit messages are user-actionable; resume across restart works via the `.claude-binding.json` sidecar.
- **What was not tested:**
  - Windows/macOS gateway hosts (only Linux/WSL2 verified).
  - Bun runtime (Node 22 only).
  - Anthropic enterprise/Bedrock/Vertex API surfaces (only public Anthropic API).
  - Concurrent multi-account scenarios beyond 3 agents.
- **Proof limitations or environment constraints:** Author runs a single-host gateway. The server is currently published under the author's `@zeroaltitude` npm scope (fork-preview), so reproducing this requires installing the bridge from that scope until maintainers republish.
- **Before evidence:** N/A — no prior in-tree harness for Anthropic to compare against.

## Tests and validation

- Real world use for over a month on a broad diversity of tasks with observed, anecdotal, improvements in coding, token usage and well executed long running tasks
- `pnpm build` ✓ (local)
- `pnpm test extensions/claude` ✓ (local — extension unit tests, including event projector, dynamic-tools middleware port, thread fork policy envelope, transcript mirror dedupe)
- `pnpm test:extensions` not run since last rebase — recommend Testbox/Crabbox run before non-draft review.
- No `CHANGELOG.md` edit per contributor rule; maintainer to add on land.

## Risk checklist

- **Did user-visible behavior change?** Yes — new `claude-bridge` harness appears in model picker; new `/claude` slash command surface.
- **Did config, environment, or migration behavior change?** Yes — new plugin `configSchema` (see `extensions/claude/openclaw.plugin.json`). Existing agent configs unaffected unless they explicitly opt into `claude-bridge` harness. Doctor contract added (`extensions/claude/doctor-contract-api.ts`).
- **Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change?** Yes (intentional). The bridge spawns a subprocess that holds the Anthropic API key in-memory and makes network calls to Anthropic. Subprocess lifecycle is owned by the harness, not user shell. The plugin SDK enabler changes (e.g. `command-registration.ts`) are additive and follow existing codex-extension precedent.
- **Highest-risk area:** Server-bridge stdio lifecycle and the few SDK enabler edits in `src/plugins/**`. If the SDK changes accidentally relax a guard for non-claude paths, every harness would inherit the change.
- **How is that risk mitigated?** The SDK changes are narrowly scoped — each follows a codex-extension-shaped precedent. Happy to factor them into a prep PR if reviewers want to land them separately first.

## Current review state

- **Next action:** Maintainer steer on scope: (a) accept as bundled extension as-is, (b) split SDK enablers into a prep PR first, or (c) request packaging as an external plugin.
- **Still waiting on:** Maintainer decision on the above. CI run on this push.
- **Bot/reviewer comments addressed:** None yet — opening draft.

---

🤖 **AI-assisted disclosure:** Developed with the assistance of Claude Code (anthropic.com/claude-code). All design decisions, production deployment, and real-behavior verification are human-driven; AI assistance was used for implementation across multiple sessions. Session transcripts available on request.
