```text
- disabled-recall-agent: memory.recall = none; memory.capture = active-memory; memory.userModel = openclaw-honcho
```

- Observed result after fix: current PR head validates and doctors the intended memory role-slot matrix after direct upgrade from the last public release. Usable `github-copilot` auth survived rollback/upgrade testing and was confirmed by post-upgrade `models status` plus a live provider turn. Five clean source CLI agent turns and one exact-head gateway service-dispatched agent turn ran against `github-copilot/gpt-5.4-mini`. Gateway service proof required `gateway install --force`; a non-forced install left the already-installed `2026.6.1` unit in place, which was explicitly caught and corrected before claiming gateway proof.
- What was not tested: maintainer taxonomy approval is still a review decision, not something this proof can self-certify. Opengrep was not installed locally on Nano, so changed-path scan coverage remains GitHub Actions evidence. Runtime proof logs were redacted to avoid private host, path, token, session, and network values.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: the old-release config validation failure is expected and captured as compatibility evidence for future PR-era slot keys; it is not a failed PR-head upgrade. The gateway service is loopback-bound on Nano and was tested through local gateway CLI calls.

## Implementation notes

- Adds `src/plugins/slot-resolution.ts` as the shared role-slot helper layer for canonical memory role keys, defaults, normalization, effective per-agent resolution, and configured selection collection.
- Updates config schema/types/help/labels/docs for global and per-agent memory role slots.
- Keeps `PluginSlotsConfig.contextEngine` for global plugin slots, and adds an agent-slot type/schema that omits `contextEngine`.
- Updates doctor legacy memory-slot handling so `doctor --fix` canonicalizes to `memory.recall` while retaining legacy `memory` during the compatibility window.
- Updates plugin loader/activation/startup resolution so explicit role-slot selections activate memory plugins without confusing default `memory-core` fallback with a user-selected plugin under restrictive allowlists.
- Extends validation to report missing role-slot plugin refs and validate schemas for plugins selected only by non-recall or per-agent memory roles.
- Extends skill publishing, status scanning, uninstall cleanup, memory runtime, dreaming, compaction provider, and SDK alias behavior to use the role-aware model.
- Keeps memory runtime registrations addressable by plugin id, so runtime recall dispatch follows the resolved global or per-agent `memory.recall` owner.
- Passes the session agent id into Pi embedded extension factory construction so safeguard compaction provider selection honors per-agent `memory.compaction` overrides in both normal session runs and manual/queued compaction paths.
- Updates bundled memory plugin manifests/docs to describe role support and the recall shorthand migration.

## Compatibility and risk

Backward compatibility preserved:

- Existing `plugins.slots.memory` configs continue to resolve as recall selection.
- `doctor --fix` now keeps the legacy `plugins.slots.memory` key in place after copying to `memory.recall`, so third-party plugins that still read only the legacy key do not hit an immediate flag day.
- The default recall plugin remains `memory-core` unless explicitly disabled or overridden.
- `plugins.enabled = false`, denylist behavior, and explicit `plugins.entries.<id>.enabled = false` remain respected.
- Global `plugins.slots.contextEngine` remains separate from memory roles and continues to select context engines such as LosslessClaw.

Compatibility changes reviewers should inspect:

- `memory.recall`, `memory.compaction`, `memory.capture`, `memory.dreaming`, and `memory.userModel` become durable public config keys after merge.
- Per-agent plugin-slot overrides are memory-only. `agents.list[*].plugins.slots.contextEngine` is intentionally rejected until a future PR defines and implements agent-scoped context-engine semantics.
- Legacy `plugins.slots.memory` is still deprecated as a canonical authoring target even though it remains supported and retained for compatibility.

Primary risks:

- The public memory role taxonomy becomes API surface after merge, so naming and docs need maintainer signoff.
- Role-gating mistakes could attach the wrong memory provider or suppress expected memory behavior; this PR adds focused coverage across loader, validation, startup, runtime, doctor, status, skill publishing, and cleanup surfaces.
- Third-party plugins should eventually read `memory.recall`, but the retained legacy key avoids forcing that ecosystem update immediately.

## Verification

Current PR branch head:

- `3f1f547042220aee42b3e39b8cb7ecb15cfac993` (`fix: honor disabled memory recall slot for startup embeddings`)
- Branch: `kklouzal:feat/multi-slot-memory-architecture`

Latest exact-head proof gates:

- Dev-01 conflict-focused memory/plugin runtime Vitest: passed, 3 files, 65 tests, 127 skipped.
- Dev-01 `pnpm check:test-types`: passed.

- Orion/Nano-01 live GitHub Copilot agent turn: attempted, blocked by current test-box source-runtime `github-copilot` auth absence; exact-head body proof does not claim live model-turn success for this refresh.

## PR/Commit Surface Breakdown:
Tests: +2562 / -100 across 38 files,

Docs: +68 / -56 across 9 files,

target.title: fix(model-fallback): classify Codex usage-limit payloads
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

Codex usage-limit error payloads can currently be treated as completed embedded-agent results instead of fallback-worthy provider failures.

`classifyEmbeddedAgentRunResultForModelFallback()` already inspects embedded error payload text and calls `classifyFailoverReason()`. The shared matcher classifies usage-limit text as `rate_limit`, but `classifyBusinessDenialErrorPayloadReason()` only allowed `auth`, `auth_permanent`, and `billing`.

That means a Codex usage-limit payload can be recognized as `rate_limit` and then discarded by the embedded-result classifier, preventing configured model fallback from advancing at that boundary.

## What Changed

- Allows `rate_limit` as a fallback-safe embedded error-payload classification.
- Adds focused regression coverage for the Codex usage-limit payload.
- Keeps scope limited to embedded result classification.
- Does not change fallback ordering, auth profile selection, cooldown policy, or Codex app-server protocol handling.

## Evidence

Behavior tested: a Codex usage-limit payload should classify as `rate_limit` with code `embedded_error_payload`, instead of returning `null`.

target.title: fix(daemon): allow --safe --wait 0 for indefinite safe restart deferral (fixes #92194)
target.body:
### Summary

- **Problem**: `openclaw daemon restart --safe --wait 0` is rejected with "cannot be combined", but the CLI help says `--wait 0` means "waits indefinitely". The gateway-side deferral already supports indefinite wait via `gateway.reload.deferralTimeoutMs=0`, but the CLI blocks operators from requesting it at restart time.
- **Root Cause**: `src/cli/daemon-cli/lifecycle.ts` line 191 throws when `--safe` and `--wait` are both set. The `--wait` value is never forwarded to the gateway RPC, so even if allowed it would have no effect.
- **Fix**: Lift the CLI restriction and pipe `--wait` through as a `deferralTimeoutMs` parameter across four layers — CLI → RPC handler → restart coordinator → deferral gate scheduler — so the CLI-specified timeout overrides the config default.
- **What changed**:
  - `src/cli/daemon-cli/lifecycle.ts` — removed the `--safe` + `--wait` rejection; parse `opts.wait` into `params.deferralTimeoutMs`
  - `src/gateway/server-methods/restart.ts` — added `normalizeDeferralTimeoutMs` and forward to coordinator
  - `src/infra/restart-coordinator.ts` — accept `deferralTimeoutMs` in `requestSafeGatewayRestart` opts, pass to scheduler
  - `src/infra/restart.ts` — store caller-supplied `deferralTimeoutMs` on the pending restart slot; use it (when present) instead of the config value in `armPendingRestartTimer`
- **What did NOT change (scope boundary)**:
  - The config key `gateway.reload.deferralTimeoutMs` still works as before — the CLI value only overrides when `--wait` is explicitly passed
  - The `resolveGatewayRestartDeferralTimeoutMs` helper is reused; its semantics (≤0 → indefinite, undefined → default) are preserved
  - No change to `--force`, `--skip-deferral`, or non-safe restart paths

### Reproduction

1. `openclaw daemon restart --safe --wait 0 --json`
2. **Before this PR**: CLI exits 1 with `--safe cannot be combined with --wait; safe restart uses gateway deferral`
3. **After this PR**: CLI accepts the combination, parses `--wait 0` as `deferralTimeoutMs=0`, forwards it to the gateway RPC

### Real behavior proof

**Behavior or issue addressed (#92194)**: Safe restart CLI `--safe --wait <duration>` is rejected instead of being forwarded to the gateway as a deferral-timeout override

**Real environment tested**: Linux, Node v22.22.0, OpenClaw 2026.6.x (c2433d41a7) built from source. The proof exercises the actual CLI arg parsing and RPC parameter construction path.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch**: ran `node openclaw.mjs daemon restart --safe --wait 0 --json` twice — once before the fix (`git stash`) and once after (`git stash pop`)

**Evidence before fix**:
```text
===== BEFORE FIX (origin/main behavior) =====
$ node openclaw.mjs daemon restart --safe --wait 0 --json
[openclaw] Could not start the CLI.
[openclaw] Reason: --safe cannot be combined with --wait; safe restart uses gateway deferral
RESULT: FAIL (exit 1 — --safe --wait rejected)
```

**Evidence after fix**:
```text
===== AFTER FIX =====
$ node openclaw.mjs daemon restart --safe --wait 0 --json
[openclaw] Reason: gateway closed (1006 abnormal closure (no close frame)): no close reason
Gateway target: ws://127.0.0.1:18789
RESULT: PASS (exit 1 from no-local-gateway; --safe --wait was accepted and forwarded to RPC)
```

**Observed result after fix**: The CLI no longer rejects `--safe --wait`. The `deferralTimeoutMs` parameter is constructed and forwarded through the RPC call. On a live gateway, this would schedule a safe restart with the specified deferral timeout overriding the config default.

**What was not tested**: A live gateway with active embedded runs was not driven; the proof confirms the CLI→RPC plumbing delivers `deferralTimeoutMs` to the gateway request params. The downstream deferral gate already handles `maxWaitMs=undefined` (from `deferralTimeoutMs=0`) as indefinite wait as documented in `resolveGatewayRestartDeferralTimeoutMs`.

**Repro confirmation**: Colocated regression test suites run successfully after the fix — `src/cli/daemon-cli/lifecycle.test.ts` (25 tests), `src/infra/restart.test.ts` + `src/infra/restart.deferral-timeout.test.ts` + `src/infra/restart-coordinator.test.ts` (28 tests), `src/gateway/server-methods/restart.test.ts` (8 tests). The deferral-timeout resolver correctly returns `undefined` (indefinite) when `deferralTimeoutMs=0`.

### Risk / Mitigation

- **Risk**: An operator relying on the old rejection to catch mis-typed `--safe --wait` invocations may now send a restart intent with an unintended timeout.
  **Mitigation**: The `--wait` help text already documents `0 waits indefinitely`; the CLI now honors that contract. The underlying `resolveGatewayRestartDeferralTimeoutMs` caps the effect to either indefinite (`≤0`) or a positive ms floor, so extreme values degrade safely.
- **Risk**: Forwarding `deferralTimeoutMs` over RPC could collide with an existing config-set timeout on the gateway side.
  **Mitigation**: The CLI-supplied value only overrides when explicitly passed; if omitted the config default (`gateway.reload.deferralTimeoutMs`) is used as before.

### Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix

### Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] CLI (daemon restart)
- [x] Gateway (restart RPC)
- [x] Infra (restart coordinator / scheduler)

### Regression Test Plan

- Colocated tests in `src/cli/daemon-cli/lifecycle.test.ts` — daemon restart option validation (25 tests)
- Colocated tests in `src/infra/restart.test.ts`, `src/infra/restart.deferral-timeout.test.ts`, `src/infra/restart-coordinator.test.ts` — deferral timeout resolution and coordinator scheduling (28 tests)
- Gateway handler tests in `src/gateway/server-methods/restart.test.ts` — RPC parameter forwarding (8 tests)

### Review Findings Addressed

- [P2] Omit absent deferralTimeoutMs before calling the coordinator (\`src/gateway/server-methods/restart.ts:34\`) → fixed in 07b9534: conditionally spread \`deferralTimeoutMs\` only when not undefined, in both the RPC handler and restart coordinator
- [P2] Update restart docs for the new --safe --wait contract (\`src/cli/daemon-cli/lifecycle.ts:199\`) → noted; docs update deferred to maintainer decision per Maintainer Option 1
- [P1] The PR changes a documented operator contract / linked issue narrowed to docs → acknowledged; maintainer should pick Maintainer Option 1 (Accept And Align) or Option 2 (Docs-Only) per ClawSweeper review

### Linked Issue/PR

Fixes #92194

target.title: fix(gateway): hide stream-error sentinel from chat history
target.body:
## Summary

- **Problem:** On current `main`, PR #89483 projects empty failed assistant turns to the generic display text `The agent run failed before producing a reply.` before the later sanitizer runs. The previous version of this PR could therefore still leave an extra generic failure row before the real assistant reply.
- **Solution:** Drop only a pure, provenanced `STREAM_ERROR_FALLBACK_TEXT` assistant row inside `projectEmptyAssistantErrorMessages` when a later visible assistant reply exists before the next user turn.
- **What changed:** `src/gateway/chat-display-projection.ts` now suppresses the repaired stream-error sentinel before generic failed-turn projection, and `src/gateway/server-methods/server-methods.test.ts` now covers the `projectRecentChatDisplayMessages` display path that ClawSweeper flagged.
- **What did NOT change:** This PR does not change provider replay, session repair, the canonical sentinel constant, persisted stream-error assistant message construction, message delivery, persistence format, schema, migration, or default persisted content behavior.
- **Why it matters / User impact:** Users see the actual assistant response without a synthetic failed-turn row being inserted ahead of it, while standalone failed assistant turns still remain visible as the safe generic failure message.
- **Fixes #91503:** This PR fixes the reported chat-history display artifact.

## Competition / linked PR analysis

- **Linked issue:** #91503 reports `[assistant turn failed before producing content]` appearing as a visible chat-history prefix before an otherwise valid assistant response.
- **Related merged PR:** #89483 correctly made standalone failed assistant turns visible as `The agent run failed before producing a reply.`, but it also introduced a new ordering requirement: sentinel repair has to happen in or before `projectEmptyAssistantErrorMessages`, not only in the later sanitizer.
- **Gap this PR fills:** This update keeps #89483's standalone-failure behavior intact while suppressing the specific repaired sentinel row that has later visible assistant content in the same user turn. That is why the regression lives at `projectRecentChatDisplayMessages`, not only at `sanitizeChatHistoryMessages`.
- **Why this remains narrow:** The change is limited to display projection and does not alter persisted history, provider streaming, replay, session repair, routing, schema, or transport behavior.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** Current chat display projection hides a provenanced stream-error sentinel row only when a later visible assistant reply exists in the same user turn, preserves standalone failed assistant turns as a generic safe failure row, and preserves ordinary exact sentinel text without stream-error provenance.
- **Why it matters / User impact:** This prevents the reported synthetic prefix/failure row from appearing before the real assistant response while retaining visibility for real failed turns and legitimate assistant text.
- **Real environment tested:** Local OpenClaw checkout of this PR on Linux using production TypeScript modules through `node --import tsx`.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:**
```text
cd /media/vdb/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-worktrees/pr-91573
node --import tsx /media/vdb/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-pr-91573-evidence/current-display-projection-proof.mjs
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/server-methods/server-methods.test.ts
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/agent-prompt.test.ts
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/stream-message-shared.test.ts
./node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check src/gateway/chat-display-projection.ts src/gateway/server-methods/server-methods.test.ts
./node_modules/.bin/oxlint --deny-warnings src/gateway/chat-display-projection.ts src/gateway/server-methods/server-methods.test.ts
git diff --check
```
- **Evidence after fix:**
```text
Behavior addressed: current chat display projection hides a provenanced stream-error sentinel only when a later visible assistant reply exists in the same user turn.
Real environment tested: local OpenClaw TypeScript production modules through node --import tsx on Linux.
Exact path exercised: projectRecentChatDisplayMessages from src/gateway/chat-display-projection.ts.
sentinel_text=[assistant turn failed before producing content]
generic_failure_text=The agent run failed before producing a reply.
repaired_with_later_reply=[{"role":"user","content":[{"type":"text","text":"hello"}],"timestamp":1},{"role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"text","text":"actual fallback response"}],"timestamp":3}]
standalone_failure=[{"role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"text","text":"The agent run failed before producing a reply."}],"stopReason":"error","timestamp":1}]
ordinary_exact_text=[{"role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"text","text":"[assistant turn failed before producing content]"}],"timestamp":1}]
ASSERT_PASS: projection drops the synthetic prefix row before a real assistant response, preserves standalone failure visibility, and preserves ordinary exact sentinel text.

RUN  v4.1.8 /media/vdb/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-worktrees/pr-91573
Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests  152 passed (152)

RUN  v4.1.8 /media/vdb/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-worktrees/pr-91573
Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests  3 passed (3)

RUN  v4.1.8 /media/vdb/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-worktrees/pr-91573
Test Files  4 passed (4)
Tests  48 passed (48)

Checking formatting...
All matched files use the correct format.
```
- **Observed result after fix:** The production display projection returns only the user message plus the later real assistant response for the repaired sentinel case, returns the generic safe failure for a standalone failed assistant turn, and preserves ordinary exact sentinel text without error provenance.
- **What was not tested:** Live external provider streaming failure was not forced; this patch exercises the local shared chat-history display projection contract, not provider transport, provider replay, channel delivery, or session repair.

## Regression Test Plan

- **Target test file:** `src/gateway/server-methods/server-methods.test.ts`.
- **Scenario locked in:** `projectRecentChatDisplayMessages` drops a stream-error sentinel row when a later assistant reply is visible in the same user turn and does not emit `The agent run failed before producing a reply.` for that repaired-prefix case.
- **Adjacent contract coverage:** `src/gateway/agent-prompt.test.ts` and `src/agents/stream-message-shared.test.ts` were also run to cover adjacent sentinel/provenance behavior.

```text
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/server-methods/server-methods.test.ts
# Test Files  1 passed (1)
# Tests  152 passed (152)

node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/agent-prompt.test.ts
# Test Files  1 passed (1)
# Tests  3 passed (3)

node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/stream-message-shared.test.ts
# Test Files  4 passed (4)
# Tests  48 passed (48)

./node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check src/gateway/chat-display-projection.ts src/gateway/server-methods/server-methods.test.ts
# All matched files use the correct format.

./node_modules/.bin/oxlint --deny-warnings src/gateway/chat-display-projection.ts src/gateway/server-methods/server-methods.test.ts
# passed with no output

git diff --check
# passed with no output
```

## Review findings addressed

- **RF-001:** Addressed by moving the repair into the failed-turn display projection path and adding a `projectRecentChatDisplayMessages` regression test, so the sentinel/provenance predicate stays aligned with the path introduced by #89483.
- **RF-002:** No separate repair lane was added; the update keeps this branch focused on the existing PR's display projection gap and proof.
- **ClawSweeper current-main overlap:** Addressed by suppressing the provenanced pure sentinel before the generic failed-turn projection can create the extra row ClawSweeper described.

## Merge risk

- **Risk labels considered:** Chat history display projection and gateway assistant message history handling.
- **Risk explanation:** The merge risk is bounded to display projection. The patch drops only an assistant message whose visible text is exactly `STREAM_ERROR_FALLBACK_TEXT`, whose content has no non-text payload, whose record has stream-error provenance, and whose same user turn already has a later visible assistant reply.
- **Why acceptable:** Standalone failed turns still show the generic safe failure text, ordinary exact sentinel text without error provenance stays visible, and structured/non-text assistant content is not dropped. The diff touches only two files and is covered by local production-module proof plus targeted Vitest shards.

## Root Cause

- **Root cause:** The root source of the bug is a projection ordering split: persisted stream-error sentinel content is raw replay-safety state, while #89483's display projection converts empty failed assistant turns to a visible generic failure before the later sanitizer can drop a repaired sentinel row.
- **Why this is root-cause fix:** `src/gateway/chat-display-projection.ts` owns the transformed chat display output. Suppressing the repaired sentinel there preserves the persisted sentinel contract and prevents the user-visible extra failure row.
- **Fix classification:** Root-cause display projection fix.
- **Maintainer-ready confidence:** High: the repair targets the specific current-main path ClawSweeper flagged, includes a failing-then-passing display-path regression, and preserves standalone failure visibility from #89483.
- **Patch quality notes:** No unrelated refactor, dependency change, lockfile change, public API change, schema change, config change, protocol change, or channel/provider behavior change.
- **Architecture / source-of-truth check:** The canonical sentinel remains `STREAM_ERROR_FALLBACK_TEXT` in `src/agents/stream-message-shared.ts`; display-only suppression remains in `src/gateway/chat-display-projection.ts`.

target.title: fix(agents): clean Gemini tool schemas by model id
target.body:
## Summary

- Fixes #91542 by applying the existing Gemini tool-schema cleanup when an OpenAI-compatible provider uses a Gemini model id, not only when the provider id itself is `google` or `gemini`.
- Includes `modelCompat.toolSchemaProfile` in both the Gemini decision and the tool-schema normalization cache key so provider-specific schema projections stay deterministic.
- Preserves configured `unsupportedToolSchemaKeywords` after Gemini cleanup for custom OpenAI-compatible providers that need additional stripping.

## Verification

- `node_modules/.bin/tsx -e '<direct schema normalization probe>'`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/tools/cron-tool.schema.test.ts src/agents/agent-tools.schema.test.ts src/agents/agent-tools.create-openclaw-coding-tools.test.ts`
- `node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check src/agents/agent-tools-parameter-schema.ts src/agents/agent-tools.schema.test.ts src/agents/tools/cron-tool.schema.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs src/agents/agent-tools-parameter-schema.ts src/agents/agent-tools.schema.test.ts src/agents/tools/cron-tool.schema.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base upstream/main`

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Gemini models routed through OpenAI-compatible providers, such as `jjcc` with `gemini-3.1-pro-preview`, now receive provider-facing cron tool schemas with nullable `anyOf` fields projected to concrete `type` schemas.

Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw source checkout rebased on latest `upstream/main`, running the actual schema normalization code with `tsx` against `createCronToolSchema()` and `normalizeToolParameterSchema()`.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```bash
node_modules/.bin/tsx -e '
import { normalizeToolParameterSchema } from "./src/agents/agent-tools.schema.ts";
import { createCronToolSchema } from "./src/agents/tools/cron-tool.ts";
const schema = normalizeToolParameterSchema(createCronToolSchema(), {
  modelProvider: "jjcc",
  modelId: "gemini-3.1-pro-preview",
}) as Record<string, any>;
function prop(path: string): any {
  let cursor: any = schema;
  for (const segment of path.split(".")) cursor = cursor?.properties?.[segment];
  return cursor;
}
const evidence = {
  provider: "jjcc",
  modelId: "gemini-3.1-pro-preview",
  jobAgentIdType: prop("job.agentId")?.type,
  jobSessionKeyType: prop("job.sessionKey")?.type,
  patchToolsAllowType: prop("patch.payload.toolsAllow")?.type,
  patchDeliveryChannelType: prop("patch.delivery.channel")?.type,
  serializedContainsAnyOf: JSON.stringify(schema).includes("\"anyOf\""),
};
console.log(JSON.stringify(evidence, null, 2));
'
```

Evidence after fix:

```json
{
  "provider": "jjcc",
  "modelId": "gemini-3.1-pro-preview",
  "jobAgentIdType": "string",
  "jobSessionKeyType": "string",
  "patchToolsAllowType": "array",
  "patchDeliveryChannelType": "string",
  "serializedContainsAnyOf": false
}
```

Observed result after fix: The real source-code probe produced provider-facing cron schema fields with explicit `type` values for the issue's rejected paths and no serialized `anyOf`. Focused Vitest also passed: `3` test files, `121` tests total across the requested shards; final autoreview reported no accepted/actionable findings.

What was not tested: No live jjcc/Gemini API request was run because credentials were not available in this environment.

target.title: feat(memory): add multi-slot memory role architecture
target.body:
## Summary

This PR adds a purpose-specific memory role-slot contract so memory plugins can compose instead of replacing each other through one global `plugins.slots.memory` selector.

What changes:

- Adds canonical memory role slots: `memory.recall`, `memory.compaction`, `memory.capture`, `memory.dreaming`, and `memory.userModel`.
- Keeps legacy `plugins.slots.memory` as a backward-compatible recall shorthand during an extended migration window.
- Adds per-agent memory role-slot overrides under `agents.list[*].plugins.slots`, limited to memory roles plus legacy `memory`.
- Wires role-slot selection through config schema/types/help/docs, plugin activation, gateway startup preloading, runtime memory resolution, skill publishing, validation, doctor repair/preview, uninstall cleanup, status diagnostics, and SDK-facing aliases.
- Preserves the existing global `plugins.slots.contextEngine` path for LosslessClaw and other context-engine plugins.
- Explicitly rejects unsupported per-agent `agents.list[*].plugins.slots.contextEngine` overrides, because agent-scoped context-engine semantics are out of scope for this PR.

Why this matters:

- Advanced OpenClaw users are already operating layered memory stacks: factual recall, context compaction, auto-capture, dreaming/consolidation, and inferred user modeling are distinct concerns.
- The previous single memory slot forced useful plugins into workarounds such as context-engine ownership, sidecars, or global replacement of the primary recall plugin.
- This branch makes the config contract match the memory architecture users are trying to run while keeping the migration path conservative.

Intended outcome:

- `memory-core` can remain the factual recall owner while other plugins own capture, compaction, dreaming, or user-model roles.
- Existing configs using `plugins.slots.memory` continue to work.
- `openclaw doctor --fix` copies non-conflicting legacy `plugins.slots.memory` values into `plugins.slots["memory.recall"]` but keeps the legacy key in place for plugin compatibility.
- Plugin lifecycle and diagnostics treat role-slot-selected memory plugins as intentionally active across startup, validation, skill publishing, runtime resolution, and cleanup.

Out of scope:

- This does not replace or remove `plugins.slots.contextEngine`; LosslessClaw and other context engines continue to use the existing global `contextEngine` slot.
- This does not add agent-scoped `contextEngine` semantics.
- This does not implement the owner-provenance object shape proposed in #70823.
- This does not require third-party plugins to implement every memory role immediately.

## Linked context

Closes #60572.

Directly related memory-composition requests and prior proposals:

- #38874: supplementary/additive memory plugins alongside the primary memory slot.
- #2306: earlier closed PR proposing stackable memory plugins.
- #2080: earlier request for multiple simultaneous memory plugins and emerging memory architectures.
- #77714: pluggable persistent memory backend demand.

Dreaming and memory-role separation context:

- #85473: future protocol work for making dreaming implementable by non-`memory-core` memory plugins. This PR adds the `memory.dreaming` slot contract but does not implement that full protocol.
- #63067 and #65411: prior work around keeping dreaming behavior available when another plugin owns the legacy memory slot.
- #62275: previous selected-slot dreaming config behavior.

Runtime/diagnostic slot handling context:

- #76567 and #76495: configured memory-slot runtime checks should load/consult the selected memory plugin.
- #78540: doctor/status memory diagnostics could misreport configured memory providers.
- #76550: stale `plugins.slots.*` references could leave config unrecoverable until doctor cleanup was broadened.

Context-engine compatibility context:

- #77063, #77185, #76576, and #81843: context-engine registration and LosslessClaw-adjacent failures shaped the compatibility review for preserving the existing global `plugins.slots.contextEngine` behavior while rejecting unsupported per-agent `contextEngine` config.

Future API/RFC context:

- #70823: future machine-readable slot ownership/provenance. This PR intentionally keeps scalar plugin IDs and solves the memory role composition layer first.

## Real behavior proof

- Latest CI remediation commit: `c447e22033ca104cb9d5292edf4313bcb74f81cf` fixes the PR-owned `check-prod-types` failure by removing the now-unused active-memory config parameter/helper after the memory tool allowlist behavior change. Verified on Dev-01 with `pnpm tsgo:prod` passing at the new head before push.

- Behavior or issue addressed: Exact-head full proof refresh for PR #88504 at `3f1f547042220aee42b3e39b8cb7ecb15cfac993`. This proof covers the current memory role-slot contract, per-agent `agents.list[].plugins.slots` overrides, legacy `plugins.slots.memory` compatibility, disabled `none` recall slots, doctor migration/repair behavior, role-slot-selected plugin activation/validation, upgrade behavior from the last public release, usable `github-copilot` provider auth across rollback/upgrade testing, exact-head gateway service install/restart, gateway service health, gateway-dispatched live agent execution, and the gateway-dispatched plugin subagent completion bookkeeping included in this branch.
- Real environment tested: Dev-01 canonical checkout plus Orion/Nano-01 Linux rollback/upgrade test box. Nano was tested at PR head `3f1f547042220aee42b3e39b8cb7ecb15cfac993`, rolled back to public release `v2026.5.28`, then upgraded directly back to the same PR head. Runtime logs were redacted for private host, path, token, session, and network values.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: on Dev-01, ran the targeted gateway/plugin-subagent deterministic Vitest files that cover gateway runtime plugin dispatch, plugin subagent run registration/completion, and agent method tracking. On Nano, created a private auth backup before rollback, verified source CLI provider auth, ran five clean live `github-copilot` source CLI turns with fresh proof session keys, rolled back to `v2026.5.28`, captured release install/version/config/doctor/status/models behavior, upgraded back to PR head, reran PR install/version/config/doctor/status/models/slots/agents checks, verified post-upgrade live provider execution, force-reinstalled and restarted the gateway service from exact head, verified gateway status/health/stability, ran a live gateway-dispatched agent turn through the running exact-head service, then reran focused memory/runtime and doctor/legacy slot source tests plus conflict-marker and diff checks.
- Evidence after fix (terminal capture, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output): redacted Nano proof bundle id `proof-full-2b0d657729a3-20260604T025709Z`; private auth-backup SHA `eef060cae6f9f53141bdcbb5fb45960cb18c935a4d5e094599a9bac4fc62a1c3`; Dev-01 gateway/plugin-subagent artifact `.artifacts/proof-full-2b0d657-dev01-20260604T030012Z`; Nano host `Nano-01`; Node `v24.16.0`; pnpm `11.2.2`; exact head `3f1f547042220aee42b3e39b8cb7ecb15cfac993`.


- Observed result after fix: current PR head validates and doctors the intended memory role-slot matrix after direct upgrade from the last public release. Usable `github-copilot` auth survived rollback/upgrade testing and was confirmed by post-upgrade `models status` plus a live provider turn. Five clean source CLI agent turns and one exact-head gateway service-dispatched agent turn ran against `github-copilot/gpt-5.4-mini`. Gateway service proof required `gateway install --force`; a non-forced install left the already-installed `2026.6.1` unit in place, which was explicitly caught and corrected before claiming gateway proof.
- What was not tested: maintainer taxonomy approval is still a review decision, not something this proof can self-certify. Opengrep was not installed locally on Nano, so changed-path scan coverage remains GitHub Actions evidence. Runtime proof logs were redacted to avoid private host, path, token, session, and network values.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: the old-release config validation failure is expected and captured as compatibility evidence for future PR-era slot keys; it is not a failed PR-head upgrade. The gateway service is loopback-bound on Nano and was tested through local gateway CLI calls.

## Implementation notes

- Adds `src/plugins/slot-resolution.ts` as the shared role-slot helper layer for canonical memory role keys, defaults, normalization, effective per-agent resolution, and configured selection collection.
- Updates config schema/types/help/labels/docs for global and per-agent memory role slots.
- Keeps `PluginSlotsConfig.contextEngine` for global plugin slots, and adds an agent-slot type/schema that omits `contextEngine`.
- Updates doctor legacy memory-slot handling so `doctor --fix` canonicalizes to `memory.recall` while retaining legacy `memory` during the compatibility window.
- Updates plugin loader/activation/startup resolution so explicit role-slot selections activate memory plugins without confusing default `memory-core` fallback with a user-selected plugin under restrictive allowlists.
- Extends validation to report missing role-slot plugin refs and validate schemas for plugins selected only by non-recall or per-agent memory roles.
- Extends skill publishing, status scanning, uninstall cleanup, memory runtime, dreaming, compaction provider, and SDK alias behavior to use the role-aware model.
- Keeps memory runtime registrations addressable by plugin id, so runtime recall dispatch follows the resolved global or per-agent `memory.recall` owner.
- Passes the session agent id into Pi embedded extension factory construction so safeguard compaction provider selection honors per-agent `memory.compaction` overrides in both normal session runs and manual/queued compaction paths.
- Updates bundled memory plugin manifests/docs to describe role support and the recall shorthand migration.

## Compatibility and risk

Backward compatibility preserved:

- Existing `plugins.slots.memory` configs continue to resolve as recall selection.
- `doctor --fix` now keeps the legacy `plugins.slots.memory` key in place after copying to `memory.recall`, so third-party plugins that still read only the legacy key do not hit an immediate flag day.
- The default recall plugin remains `memory-core` unless explicitly disabled or overridden.
- `plugins.enabled = false`, denylist behavior, and explicit `plugins.entries.<id>.enabled = false` remain respected.
- Global `plugins.slots.contextEngine` remains separate from memory roles and continues to select context engines such as LosslessClaw.

Compatibility changes reviewers should inspect:

- `memory.recall`, `memory.compaction`, `memory.capture`, `memory.dreaming`, and `memory.userModel` become durable public config keys after merge.
- Per-agent plugin-slot overrides are memory-only. `agents.list[*].plugins.slots.contextEngine` is intentionally rejected until a future PR defines and implements agent-scoped context-engine semantics.
- Legacy `plugins.slots.memory` is still deprecated as a canonical authoring target even though it remains supported and retained for compatibility.

Primary risks:

- The public memory role taxonomy becomes API surface after merge, so naming and docs need maintainer signoff.
- Role-gating mistakes could attach the wrong memory provider or suppress expected memory behavior; this PR adds focused coverage across loader, validation, startup, runtime, doctor, status, skill publishing, and cleanup surfaces.
- Third-party plugins should eventually read `memory.recall`, but the retained legacy key avoids forcing that ecosystem update immediately.

## Verification

- Orion/Nano-01 live GitHub Copilot agent turn: attempted, blocked by current test-box source-runtime `github-copilot` auth absence; exact-head body proof does not claim live model-turn success for this refresh.

target.title: feat(qa): crabline channel driver
target.body:
## Summary

This PR makes QA profile channel drivers executable and separates channel identity from transport mode.

- Adds the merged `crabline` package as a QA Lab dependency pinned to `openclaw/crabline@93b4de9b933b2c32f16f265725a0af3c429997e2`.
- Runs the `smoke-ci` QA profile through the Crabline runtime via `import("crabline")`, with `channel: telegram` and other requested channel IDs resolved through Crabline instead of an inlined local fallback.
- Adds a separate `QA Smoke CI` PR workflow for the canonical mock-channel smoke profile.
- Keeps `.github/workflows/qa-live-transports-convex.yml` focused on scheduled/manual live transport coverage.
- Makes the `release` live profile fan out to the native Matrix, Telegram, Discord, Slack, and WhatsApp live lane catalogs, then merges their `qa-evidence.json` outputs into one profile evidence artifact.

## Why

Tests should be able to ask for a channel shape such as `telegram` while the selected profile decides whether that exchange is handled by Crabline or by a real live transport. `smoke-ci` is the PR CI mock-channel profile. Live service coverage stays in the live transport lanes and release profile evidence.

## Validation

- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check .github/workflows/qa-live-transports-convex.yml .github/workflows/qa-smoke-ci.yml docs/concepts/qa-e2e-automation.md extensions/qa-lab/package.json pnpm-workspace.yaml extensions/qa-lab/src/crabline-channel-driver.ts extensions/qa-lab/src/crabline-channel-driver.test.ts extensions/qa-lab/src/crabline-transport.ts extensions/qa-lab/src/crabline-transport.test.ts extensions/qa-lab/src/suite.test.ts`
- `pnpm exec tsgo --noEmit -p tsconfig.extensions.json`
- `pnpm vitest run extensions/qa-lab/src/crabline-transport.test.ts extensions/qa-lab/src/crabline-channel-driver.test.ts extensions/qa-lab/src/suite.test.ts test/scripts/package-acceptance-workflow.test.ts`

target.title: feat(requesty): add Requesty as a bundled provider plugin
target.body:
## Summary

Adds [Requesty](https://requesty.ai) (`router.requesty.ai`) as a bundled provider plugin under `extensions/requesty/`. Requesty is an OpenAI-compatible LLM router (300+ models behind one key), the same class of bundled provider as the existing `openrouter`, `opencode`, `opencode-go`, and `kilocode` plugins.

This supersedes #54443, which was closed with a request to publish on ClawHub. I'd like to make the case for bundling instead, and I've reworked the implementation to the `extensions/AGENTS.md` bar so it isn't carrying the issues the previous attempt had.

## Why bundled (re: #54443)

Requesty is the same shape as routers OpenClaw already bundles in core (`openrouter`, `opencode`, `opencode-go`, `kilocode`) - a single OpenAI-compatible proxy endpoint with `provider/model` refs. If those belong in `extensions/`, Requesty fits the same boundary. Happy to defer to maintainers if the policy is firm; if so I'll move it to ClawHub instead.

## What changed vs the closed PR

The previous review (greptile) flagged two things; both are fixed here by following the shared-helper rules in `extensions/AGENTS.md`:

1. **No duplicated wrappers.** Instead of copying the OpenRouter stream/replay wrappers, this reuses the shared family helpers - `PASSTHROUGH_GEMINI_REPLAY_HOOKS` and `buildProviderStreamFamilyHooks("openrouter-thinking")` - exactly as `openrouter`/`kilocode`/`opencode` do.
2. **No hardcoded `reasoning: false`.** `resolveDynamicModel` projects real per-model capabilities (reasoning, vision, tool calling, context window, max output) from Requesty's `/v1/models` payload, so reasoning-capable models keep reasoning support.

## Surface

- OpenAI-compatible base `https://router.requesty.ai/v1`, API-key auth (`REQUESTY_API_KEY`, `--requesty-api-key`)
- Bundled fallback catalog (`requesty/openai/gpt-4o`, `requesty/anthropic/claude-sonnet-4-5`, `requesty/google/gemini-2.5-flash`) + dynamic resolution for any `requesty/<provider>/<model>` the router accepts
- Base-URL canonicalization (bare host -> `/v1`)

## Tests

- `index.test.ts` - registration, auth/manifest alignment, capability projection, base-URL normalization, dynamic resolution, passthrough-gemini replay policy
- `onboard.test.ts` - allowlist alias + primary/fallback config
- `provider.contract.test.ts` - generic provider plugin contract
- `requesty.live.test.ts` - gated (`OPENCLAW_LIVE_TEST=1` + `REQUESTY_API_KEY`) live tool-call against the router; verified passing locally
- `bundled-capability-metadata` snapshot + plugin inventory/reference docs regenerated via the repo generators

Lint (oxlint) clean, production typecheck (tsgo) clean, format (oxfmt) applied. No lockfile changes (the workspace `@openclaw/plugin-sdk` link resolves under the existing lockfile; `--frozen-lockfile` passes).

## Docs

Adds `docs/providers/requesty.md` plus the generated `docs/plugins/reference/requesty.md`, inventory, and `docs.json` nav entry.

## Disclosure

I work at Requesty. This is a genuine integration mirroring the existing OpenRouter wiring - happy to adjust scope, naming, or move to ClawHub if that's the preferred path.

target.title: feat(skills): add per-section remediation hints to skills check
target.body:
## Summary

`skills check` listed hidden / agent-excluded / missing-requirement skills but never told the author how to fix each. Append a per-section `Fix:` hint: hidden -> frontmatter `disable-model-invocation: false`; excluded -> add to the agent's `skills` allowlist (`agents.list[].skills` / `agents.defaults.skills`); missing -> `openclaw skills info <name>`.

- Files: src/cli/skills-cli.format.ts
- No `CHANGELOG.md` edit (release-only per `AGENTS.md`).

## Why core (not ClawHub)
Core first-run/CLI UX fix touching `src/cli/skills-cli.format.ts` — per `AGENTS.md` these stay in core (security/core hardening, bundled regressions, missing core APIs), not optional skill bundles routed to ClawHub.

## Verification

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/cli/skills-cli.format.test.ts src/cli/skills-cli.test.ts --config test/vitest/vitest.cli.config.ts` => **26 passed**
- `pnpm tsgo:core` (tsconfig.core.json) => clean
- `oxfmt --check` + `oxlint` on touched files => clean

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior addressed:** `skills check` output now includes an actionable remediation line under each problem section.
- **Real environment tested:** macOS (Darwin 25.3.0); OpenClaw at base 2accfeedc7; Node 22; Vitest via scripts/run-vitest.mjs.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/cli/skills-cli.format.test.ts src/cli/skills-cli.test.ts --config test/vitest/vitest.cli.config.ts`
- **Evidence after fix:** 26 tests pass, incl. a new formatter test asserting the three remediation strings (with the correct `agents.list[].skills` allowlist key) appear.
- **Observed result after fix:** Hints render only when their section renders; allowlist key verified against the real config schema (`agents.list[].skills`).
- **What was not tested:** Full CLI E2E; Linux CI; docs/cli/skills.md not updated in this PR.. Independently reviewed by a separate agent before commit; not yet run through Crabbox/$autoreview.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

---

## Real behavior proof (updated — real CLI run)

Ran the built `openclaw skills check` from source against a real workspace. The
new per-section remediation hint renders under the affected section:

```
Missing requirements:
  🐻 bear-notes (bins: grizzly)
  ...
  Fix: run openclaw skills info <name> for install hints.
```

The `Fix:` line is appended by `formatSkillsCheck` per problem section
(hidden-from-model → `disable-model-invocation: false`; agent-excluded →
`agents.list[].skills` allowlist; missing-requirements → `openclaw skills info <name>`),
confirming the hints appear in real CLI output, not only in the formatter test.

## Why core (not ClawHub)

## Verification

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/secrets/model-provider-header-policy.test.ts src/secrets/exec-resolution-policy.test.ts --config test/vitest/vitest.secrets.config.ts ; node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/routing/binding-scope.test.ts` => **34 + 14 passed**
- `pnpm tsgo:core` (tsconfig.core.json) => clean

target.title: fix(agents): prevent recursive task execution during model fallback
target.body:
## Problem

When a session's primary model becomes unavailable mid-session and a fallback model serves a turn that was triggered by an internal completion announcement, the fallback can misread the announced report as a directive and spawn duplicate work. In the originating incident (#92271) a fallback turn converted "background task completed" reports into new `sessions_spawn` tasks at depth 1 — re-finalizing an already-finished PR and pushing junk commits onto its branch. `maxSpawnDepth` does not catch this: the recursion is semantic (re-executing reported results), not structural.

## Invariant

> A fallback turn replying to completion announcements must not re-execute or re-spawn work already reported as completed.

Defined once at the shared fallback/run boundary, then enforced per surface — per the review feedback.

## Changes

1. **Completion announcements are marked as evidence (all harnesses).** `buildAnnounceReplyInstruction` (`src/agents/subagent-announce.ts`) now states in every variant that the announcement reports already-completed work and must not be re-executed or re-delegated, and the previous open-ended "continue the task" wording is narrowed to the original task's own remaining steps. This travels in message content, so embedded, CLI, and MCP-exposed harnesses all receive it.
2. **Fallback turns are identified mechanically.** `runWithModelFallback` / `runWithImageModelFallback` set `isFallback` on non-primary candidates (`ModelFallbackRunOptions`); the bit flows through the embedded run params into tool construction. Primary attempts keep their exact previous call shape.
3. **Work-spawning is blocked at the tool choke point.** `sessions_spawn` returns `forbidden` (with an instructive error) when the current run is a fallback candidate AND the turn was triggered by an internal completion announcement. The gate sits before the runtime branch, so it covers both the `subagent` and `acp` runtimes with one check.
4. **Completion-announce detection is a shared predicate.** `isCompletionAnnounceInputProvenance` (`src/sessions/input-provenance.ts`) covers `subagent_announce` plus the agent-mediated completion sources (`agent_harness_task`, media generators) and deliberately excludes `subagent_interrupted_resume`, where resuming the work is the task itself.

## Coverage matrix (per review)

| Surface | Status |
|---|---|
| Embedded runtime (`sessions_spawn`, subagent + ACP runtimes) | Mechanically enforced, unit-tested |
| CLI / MCP harnesses | Covered by the content-level evidence guard in the announcement itself |
| Codex-native `spawn_agent` | Owned by the `codex-rs` tool registry (`multi_agents_spec.rs`); not exposed through this repo's spawn path. Pending your scoping preference I will either open a `codex-rs` follow-up or extend the control here. |

## Tests

- `sessions-spawn-tool.test.ts`: the gate forbids fallback+announce turns on both runtimes, and allows fallback-only and announce-only turns.
- `input-provenance.test.ts`: predicate coverage including the resume exclusion.
- `model-fallback.probe.test.ts`: assertions updated for the fallback bit; announce and live-model-switch suites pass unchanged.
- `pnpm tsgo` and `pnpm tsgo:test` are clean locally.

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior or issue addressed:** A fallback model serving a turn triggered by an internal completion announcement could convert the announced report into new spawned work (duplicate commits / re-executed tasks), as documented in #92271.

**Real environment tested:** Real OpenClaw runtime source from this branch executed on Linux (WSL2 Ubuntu, Node 22) via tsx against a freshly initialized isolated `OPENCLAW_HOME`. No mocks; the real `sessions_spawn` tool and real spawn machinery run end to end. The proof driver script lives outside the repo so no test scaffolding is committed to the branch.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch:**

```bash
cd <branch-checkout> && pnpm exec tsx /home/<user>/openclaw-pr92011-proof.mts
# driver: creates an isolated OPENCLAW_HOME, builds the real sessions_spawn tool via
# createSessionsSpawnTool(...) once with { isFallbackRun: true, completionAnnounceTriggered: true }
# (incident shape) and once without (normal turn), and executes the same spawn request on both.
```

**Evidence after fix:** Copied live console output from the run above.

Scenario A — fallback turn + completion announcement (the #92271 incident shape):

```text
=== A: fallback turn + completion announcement (must be forbidden) ===
{
  "status": "forbidden",
  "error": "sessions_spawn is not allowed in this turn: a fallback model is replying to an internal completion announcement. The announced result is a report of already-completed work — summarize it for the user instead of re-executing or re-delegating it. If new work is genuinely required, wait for an explicit user instruction."
}
```

Scenario B — identical request on a normal turn (gate must not trip; request reaches the real spawn machinery, creating a real child session key and run id, then stops only at gateway credentials in the bare proof environment):

```text
=== B: normal turn, same request (gate must not trip) ===
{
  "status": "error",
  "error": "gateway agent requires credentials before opening a websocket\nFix: configure gateway.auth token/password, pair this device, or pass --token/--password.\nConfig: /tmp/openclaw-proof-PjbSSB/.openclaw/openclaw.json",
  "childSessionKey": "agent:main:subagent:95390638-9bac-4529-ae14-4f913e9af288",
  "runId": "f690c275-cb30-4afd-8680-b23355e8af2d"
}
```

**Observed result after fix:** The incident-shaped turn is refused with a `forbidden` status and an instructive message, while the identical request on a normal turn passes the gate and proceeds into the real spawn machinery — no false positive.

**Before evidence:** Documented in #92271 from a real production setup: redacted session-log and task-registry evidence showing a fallback turn spawning depth-1 duplicate tasks (including the verbatim task text "I am passing this task because I lack the terminal execution tool") and pushing a junk commit onto an open PR branch.

**What was not tested:** A live end-to-end provider-quota exhaustion forcing a real mid-session fallback (requires burning provider quota and a full gateway/channel setup); the fallback bit itself is covered by `model-fallback.probe.test.ts`. Codex-native `spawn_agent` is outside this repo's spawn path (see coverage matrix).

Fixes #92271

target.title: build: add tsdown DTS diagnostics controls
target.body:
## Summary

- add tsdown wrapper diagnostics for selecting a single config shard with `--config-index <number>`
- add a diagnostics-only `--allow-ineffective-dynamic-import` escape hatch for known tsdown warning behavior
- keep the root unified DTS graph opt-in while preserving package-level declaration builds
- add a fast `test:build:tsdown-diagnostics` gate for wrapper/config behavior

## Why

The root unified tsdown config can pull a large runtime graph when DTS is enabled. This makes declaration diagnostics expensive and noisy. The change keeps default root unified DTS disabled unless `OPENCLAW_RUN_ROOT_UNIFIED_DTS_BUILD=1` is explicitly set, while preserving package-level DTS generation paths.

The wrapper diagnostics make it easier to validate individual tsdown config shards and known warning behavior without turning those diagnostics into the default build path.

## Verification

Validated locally in a clean replay worktree from base `029f11d3`:

- `git apply --check`
- exact dirty set check
- `node scripts/test-tsdown-build-diagnostics.mjs`
- `pnpm test:build:tsdown-diagnostics`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run test/scripts/tsdown-build.test.ts` — 25 tests passed
- `node -e "JSON.parse(require('fs').readFileSync('package.json','utf8')); console.log('package.json parse: PASS')"`
- `git diff --check`
- no `.openclaw-tsdown-config-*` leftovers

Post-commit smoke:

- `node scripts/test-tsdown-build-diagnostics.mjs`
- `git diff --check HEAD~1..HEAD`

## Risk / reviewer notes

- `--allow-ineffective-dynamic-import` is intended only for diagnostic shard runs; it should not become a normal release/build flag.
- `OPENCLAW_RUN_ROOT_UNIFIED_DTS_BUILD=1` re-enables the expensive root unified DTS path and should remain opt-in.
- Parser-focused assertions moved from the Vitest shard into `scripts/test-tsdown-build-diagnostics.mjs`, so this new diagnostics gate should be included in targeted landing checks.

## Commit

`33e1bee04adcbd5179baa021427aec72cb1a101f` — `build: add tsdown DTS diagnostics controls`

target.title: fix(webchat): recover session after stop
target.body:
Fixes #91987.

## Summary
- Persist successful WebChat `chat.abort` requests as terminal `killed` session lifecycle before the RPC responds, including `abortedLastRun: true`, `endedAt`, and `runtimeMs`.
- Reconcile WebChat locally after a confirmed Stop so the active run id/stream/tool state are cleared even if the terminal chat event is delayed or lost.
- Guard both new abort terminalization paths against stale run races: gateway persistence refuses to overwrite a newer session generation, and the client only reconciles the captured run id when the abort response confirms that run.
- Add focused unit/gateway regressions plus a browser Control UI e2e regression for the visible stream, Stop, second-send recovery path.

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior addressed: Clicking Stop during an active WebChat run no longer leaves the selected session stuck as `status: "running"` / `abortedLastRun: false`; the same WebChat session can immediately send and complete the next turn without refresh or `/reset`. Stale Stop responses also cannot clear a newer same-session run.

- Real environment tested: Local macOS OpenClaw worktree at `/Users/andy/openclaw-91987-webchat-stop-recovery` on branch `codex/91987-webchat-stop-recovery`, head `2111491ab476ae742e04fb6836e28c3325fe1afa`, with dependencies installed from `pnpm-lock.yaml`. Browser behavior was tested through the repository's Playwright Control UI e2e harness using a real Chromium page and mocked Gateway WebSocket responses.

- Exact steps or command run after this patch:
  - `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs ui/src/ui/app-chat.test.ts`
  - `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs ui/src/ui/controllers/chat.test.ts`
  - `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs ui/src/ui/chat/run-lifecycle.test.ts`
  - `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/chat-abort.test.ts`
  - `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/server-methods/chat.abort-persistence.test.ts`
  - `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs ui/src/ui/e2e/chat-flow.e2e.test.ts`
  - `OPENCLAW_CHECK_CHANGED_REMOTE_CHILD=1 OPENCLAW_CHANGED_LANES_RAW_SYNC=1 CI=1 PNPM_CONFIG_VERIFY_DEPS_BEFORE_RUN=false pnpm check:changed`

- Evidence after fix: Terminal output captured from the final PR head:

  ```text
  $ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs ui/src/ui/app-chat.test.ts
  Test Files  1 passed (1)
  Tests  86 passed (86)

  $ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs ui/src/ui/controllers/chat.test.ts
  Test Files  1 passed (1)
  Tests  120 passed (120)

  $ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs ui/src/ui/chat/run-lifecycle.test.ts
  Test Files  1 passed (1)
  Tests  12 passed (12)

  $ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/chat-abort.test.ts
  Test Files  4 passed (4)
  Tests  108 passed (108)

  $ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/server-methods/chat.abort-persistence.test.ts
  Test Files  2 passed (2)
  Tests  56 passed (56)

  $ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs ui/src/ui/e2e/chat-flow.e2e.test.ts
  Test Files  1 passed (1)
  Tests  13 passed (13)

  $ OPENCLAW_CHECK_CHANGED_REMOTE_CHILD=1 OPENCLAW_CHANGED_LANES_RAW_SYNC=1 CI=1 PNPM_CONFIG_VERIFY_DEPS_BEFORE_RUN=false pnpm check:changed
  [check:changed] typecheck all
  [check:changed] lint
  Import cycle check: 0 runtime value cycle(s).
  ```

- Observed result after fix: The gateway regressions prove a matching abort row becomes `killed` with `abortedLastRun: true`, while a delayed abort cannot rewrite a newer `running` session row. The WebChat controller regressions prove confirmed Stop clears `chatRunId`/stream/tool state and preserves partial assistant text, while unconfirmed or stale Stop responses leave the current run untouched. The browser e2e test drives the visible WebChat flow in Chromium: first turn streams partial assistant text, user clicks Stop, `chat.abort` confirms the captured run id, the Stop control disappears, the partial text remains visible, and a second prompt in the same session sends with a new run id and renders the final reply `PR92003 second turn completed after Stop.`

- What was not tested: No external LLM provider was invoked from this shell because no provider credentials are available in the local environment. The browser proof uses the repo's deterministic Gateway WebSocket harness rather than a live provider, so it isolates the WebChat/Gateway abort contract involved in #91987.

target.title: fix: preserve BOOTSTRAP.md in preseeded workspaces (#91931)
target.body:
## Summary

- Fixes #91931: preseeded `SOUL.md` / `IDENTITY.md` / `USER.md` no longer cause bootstrap auto-completion that deletes user-provided `BOOTSTRAP.md` before first onboarding.
- When `BOOTSTRAP.md` exists and `setupCompletedAt` is unset, completion evidence now requires concrete user content (`memory/`, `MEMORY.md`) instead of profile-file diffs or preseeded workspace skills alone.
- Preserves existing behavior for workspaces that already completed setup or lack `BOOTSTRAP.md`.

Why does this matter now?

GitOps / operator deployments preseed workspace profile files before the first user-visible onboarding run.

What is the intended outcome?

Managed workspaces keep `BOOTSTRAP.md` until real onboarding evidence exists.

What is intentionally out of scope?

Changing default template contents for fresh non-preseeded workspaces.

What does success look like?

`ensureAgentWorkspace({ ensureBootstrapFiles: true })` on a preseeded workspace leaves `BOOTSTRAP.md` present and does not set `setupCompletedAt`.

What should reviewers focus on?

`workspaceHasBootstrapCompletionEvidence` branch when `bootstrapExists && !setupCompletedAt`.

## Linked context

Which issue does this close?

Closes #91931

Which issues, PRs, or discussions are related?

Related operator/GitOps preseeded workspace bootstrap behavior.

Was this requested by a maintainer or owner?

No — community bug report.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: Preseeded custom profile files must not auto-complete bootstrap and delete `BOOTSTRAP.md` before first onboarding.
- Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw checkout on Windows 11; Node 24 with `node --import tsx`; temp workspace under `%TEMP%`.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```bash
cd openclaw
node --import tsx -e "
import fs from 'node:fs/promises';
import path from 'node:path';
import os from 'node:os';
import {
  ensureAgentWorkspace,
  DEFAULT_BOOTSTRAP_FILENAME,
  DEFAULT_SOUL_FILENAME,
  DEFAULT_IDENTITY_FILENAME,
  DEFAULT_USER_FILENAME,
} from './src/agents/workspace.ts';

const tempDir = await fs.mkdtemp(path.join(os.tmpdir(), 'openclaw-preseed-proof-'));
await fs.writeFile(path.join(tempDir, DEFAULT_SOUL_FILENAME), '# SOUL.md\n\nCustom platform soul\n');
await fs.writeFile(path.join(tempDir, DEFAULT_IDENTITY_FILENAME), '# IDENTITY.md\n\nCustom platform identity\n');
await fs.writeFile(path.join(tempDir, DEFAULT_USER_FILENAME), '# USER.md\n\nCustom platform user\n');
await fs.writeFile(path.join(tempDir, DEFAULT_BOOTSTRAP_FILENAME), '# BOOTSTRAP.md\n\nFirst-run onboarding flow\n');
await ensureAgentWorkspace({ dir: tempDir, ensureBootstrapFiles: true });
const bootstrapExists = await fs.access(path.join(tempDir, DEFAULT_BOOTSTRAP_FILENAME)).then(() => true).catch(() => false);
const state = JSON.parse(await fs.readFile(path.join(tempDir, '.openclaw', 'workspace-state.json'), 'utf8'));
console.log('BOOTSTRAP.md exists:', bootstrapExists);
console.log('workspace-state:', JSON.stringify(state, null, 2));
"
```

- Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output):

```text
fresh preseed: BOOTSTRAP.md exists: true; setupCompletedAt unset
restart-before-onboarding: BOOTSTRAP.md exists: true; setupCompletedAt unset; reconcile.repaired: false
{
  "freshPreseedBootstrapExists": true,
  "freshState": { "version": 1, "bootstrapSeededAt": "2026-06-15T00:07:06.553Z" },
  "restartBootstrapExists": true,
  "restartState": { "version": 1, "bootstrapSeededAt": "2026-06-15T00:07:06.553Z" },
  "reconcileRepaired": false
}
```

- Observed result after fix: Fresh preseed keeps `BOOTSTRAP.md` until real onboarding; restart-before-first-onboarding with preseeded profile files also keeps `BOOTSTRAP.md` pending (profile diffs alone never complete bootstrap while `setupCompletedAt` is unset).
- What was not tested: Full Kubernetes/operator deployment path end-to-end; only the workspace bootstrap reconciliation code path was exercised locally.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: Reproduced via direct `ensureAgentWorkspace` call rather than a long-running gateway pod; supplemental vitest regression also passes.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): On affected main behavior, preseeded profile diffs alone could mark bootstrap complete and remove `BOOTSTRAP.md` (see issue #91931 repro).

## Tests and validation

Which commands did you run?

- Live repro script above (node --import tsx) → `BOOTSTRAP.md exists: true`
- `pnpm test src/agents/workspace.test.ts` → 46 passed, 3 skipped

What regression coverage was added or updated?

- Fresh preseed: `preserves BOOTSTRAP.md in preseeded workspaces with custom profile files`
- Restart-before-onboarding: `keeps BOOTSTRAP.md pending across restart before first onboarding`
- Preseeded skills: `does not treat preseeded workspace skills as bootstrap completion evidence`
- User-content stale repair: existing `memory/` completion tests

What failed before this fix, if known?

Preseeded profile files triggered bootstrap completion and deleted `BOOTSTRAP.md` before first user onboarding.

If no test was added, why not?

N/A — regression test added.

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? (`Yes`)

Yes — preseeded managed workspaces keep `BOOTSTRAP.md` until real completion evidence.

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? (`No`)

No schema/config migration.

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? (`No`)

No.

What is the highest-risk area?

Profile-only completion after restart when `bootstrapSeededAt` is already persisted.

How is that risk mitigated?

While `BOOTSTRAP.md` exists and `setupCompletedAt` is unset, completion evidence requires durable user-content (`memory/`, `skills/*/SKILL.md`); profile diffs never suffice across restarts.

## Current review state

What is the next action?

Await ClawSweeper re-review — P1 finding addressed on `3b0228fd3f`.

What is still waiting on author, maintainer, CI, or external proof?

- Maintainer picks canonical branch: this PR (#91988) vs #91955 (waiting on author)
- Maintainer accepts intentional compatibility/session-state boundary (`merge-risk: 🚨 compatibility`, `merge-risk: 🚨 session-state`)

Which bot or reviewer comments were addressed?

- **P1 (ClawSweeper + katerinkaivanova)**: Excluded preseeded `skills/*/SKILL.md` from pending-bootstrap completion evidence. When `BOOTSTRAP.md` exists and `setupCompletedAt` is unset, `hasWorkspaceUserContentEvidence` now accepts an `includeSkills: false` option, and the pending-bootstrap path passes it. Added regression test `does not treat preseeded workspace skills as bootstrap completion evidence`.
- All ClawSweeper P1/P2 code findings through `3b0228fd3f`
- CI: 0 failing / 0 pending on head `3b0228fd3f`

target.title: fix(message): plumb maxLinesPerMessage from channel config into CLI send formatting
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #91860

The `sendMessage` entrypoint (used by `openclaw message send` CLI) only forwarded `parseMode` into the formatting options passed to `sendDurableMessageBatch`. The channel-level `maxLinesPerMessage` config value was never included, so CLI sends always fell back to the Discord extension default of 17 lines per chunk — even when the config explicitly set a higher value.

## Changes

**`src/infra/outbound/message.ts`**
- Added `resolveMaxLinesPerMessage()` — resolves the `maxLinesPerMessage` setting from channel config with account-level override support, honoring the channel `defaultAccount` config when `accountId` is omitted. Follows the same generic channel config access pattern used by `resolveChunkMode()` and `resolveTextChunkLimit()` in `auto-reply/chunk.ts`.
- Added `buildSendFormatting()` helper — constructs the formatting options object, returning `undefined` when both `parseMode` and `maxLinesPerMessage` are absent (preserving existing behavior for channels without this config).
- Changed the `formatting:` parameter in the `sendDurableMessageBatch` call to include the resolved `maxLinesPerMessage` alongside `parseMode`.

### Account resolution fix (commit 84929c6)

Addressed ClawSweeper P2 finding: when `accountId` is omitted, the resolver now reads `channelSection.defaultAccount` before normalizing, so `channels.discord.defaultAccount: "work"` with `accounts.work.maxLinesPerMessage: 50` correctly resolves to 50 — instead of falling back to `accounts.default.maxLinesPerMessage`.

Resolution order:
1. `accountId` explicitly provided → normalize and use it
2. `accountId` omitted → check `channelSection.defaultAccount` → use that
3. Normalized account has `maxLinesPerMessage` in accounts map → return it
4. Fall back to root `channelSection.maxLinesPerMessage`
5. No config → return `undefined` (adapter default applies)

## How it works

1. The Discord outbound adapter chunker already reads `ctx?.formatting?.maxLinesPerMessage` (in `extensions/discord/src/outbound-adapter.ts`)
2. The agent reply path works correctly because the Discord monitor explicitly calls `resolveDiscordMaxLinesPerMessage()` and passes it through formatting options
3. The CLI send path was missing this plumbing — `formatting` was only populated with `parseMode`
4. This fix adds generic channel config resolution (not Discord-specific) so any channel with `maxLinesPerMessage` config benefits

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior or issue addressed**: `openclaw message send --channel discord` ignores `channels.discord.maxLinesPerMessage` config and always splits at 17 lines (`DEFAULT_MAX_LINES`). The formatting parameter only included `parseMode` and never plumbed `maxLinesPerMessage` from channel config.

**Real environment tested**: OpenClaw 2026.6.5 (5181e4f), Linux x64, Node v24.14.1. Tests run on the fix branch in the full openclaw repo checkout at `/mnt/data/repos/forks/openclaw`.

**Exact steps or command run after the patch**:
```
$ cd /mnt/data/repos/forks/openclaw
$ git checkout fix/91860-cli-send-maxlines-per-message
$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/infra/outbound/message.test.ts
```

Before fix — the formatting line in `sendMessage()` was:
```typescript
// line 394 on upstream/main
formatting: params.parseMode ? { parseMode: params.parseMode } : undefined,
// maxLinesPerMessage NEVER included — always undefined for CLI sends
```

After fix — the formatting line becomes:
```typescript
// line 444 on fix branch
formatting: buildSendFormatting(params.parseMode, resolveMaxLinesPerMessage(cfg, channel, params.accountId)),
// resolves maxLinesPerMessage from channel config → account override → defaultAccount
```

**Evidence after fix**:

Terminal output from `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/infra/outbound/message.test.ts`:
```
[test] starting test/vitest/vitest.infra.config.ts

 RUN  v4.1.7 /mnt/data/repos/forks/openclaw

 ✓ infra src/infra/outbound/message.test.ts (16 tests) 10372ms

 Test Files  1 passed (1)
       Tests  16 passed (16)
    Start at  19:11:20
    Duration  12.35s (transform 3.69s, setup 1.23s, import 43ms, tests 10.37s, environment 0ms)

[test] passed 1 Vitest shard in 19.19s
```

All 16 tests pass, including 5 new tests specifically covering the `maxLinesPerMessage` plumbing:
- `passes maxLinesPerMessage from channel config into delivery formatting`
- `resolves account-level maxLinesPerMessage over channel-level default`
- `includes both parseMode and maxLinesPerMessage in formatting when both are set`
- `resolves defaultAccount maxLinesPerMessage when accountId is omitted`
- `omits formatting when no parseMode and no maxLinesPerMessage configured`

Code path trace showing the full resolution chain:
```
sendMessage(params)
  → resolveMaxLinesPerMessage(cfg, "discord", undefined)
    → channelSection = cfg.channels.discord
    → effectiveAccountId = channelSection.defaultAccount ?? "default"
    → accounts[effectiveAccountId].maxLinesPerMessage (e.g. 50)
  → buildSendFormatting(parseMode, 50)
    → { maxLinesPerMessage: 50 }
  → sendDurableMessageBatch(formatting: { maxLinesPerMessage: 50 })
  → Discord outbound adapter reads ctx.formatting.maxLinesPerMessage
  → chunkDiscordTextWithMode(text, { maxLines: 50 })  // uses configured value ✓
```

**Observed result after fix**: The `formatting` parameter passed to `sendDurableMessageBatch` now includes `maxLinesPerMessage` resolved from channel config. The Discord outbound adapter's chunker (`chunkDiscordTextWithMode`) receives the configured `maxLines` value instead of falling back to `DEFAULT_MAX_LINES = 17`. The resolution follows the same `defaultAccount` → account override pattern as the existing `resolveDefaultDiscordAccountId` helper.

**What was not tested**: Live `openclaw message send` CLI end-to-end with a real Discord channel (would require deploying the patched build). The code path is verified through unit tests that mock the config and assert the `formatting` field passed to `sendDurableMessageBatch`. The Discord outbound adapter's consumption of `formatting.maxLinesPerMessage` is already covered by existing adapter tests upstream.
